question: Accelerated Content Pathway
answer:
Accelerated Content Pathway
To break content writing into workable components, we follow an agile sprint methodology. Depending on the size of the project, a sprint will average two weeks with an assigned block of content. This is extremely beneficial to your team as content for review will come back in waves, allowing for more digestible work assignments.
An agile framework also enables shorter feedback loops, which improves speed and quality as we adjust quickly to the response from your team. Stamats recognizes that content is often the longest part of the project. Our proprietary approach called “Accelerated Content Pathway” allows content to be developed before final design, utilizing the new content strategy approach and without content rewrites.

question: Branded SEM Campaigns
answer:
Branded SEM campaigns can be tricky—sometimes serving ads where your brand already dominates Page One organically. However, competitors often bid on each other’s brand names to disrupt the consumer journey, a legal but aggressive tactic.

The key is smart monitoring. By tracking branded search competition and using Paid vs. Organic reports, we can decide when to pause ads and rely on organic clicks, or when to keep them running to push competitor ads lower on the page.

question: Privacy and Security Policies and Procedures
answer:
Cybersecurity Experience

Stamats adheres to the most rigorous data security practices for all clients. Our security measures are based on the following industry standards:

User Role Access: Restricting what users can access by roles.

Updates: Automatic updates to security issues for plug-ins and CMS platform.

Network Security: Stamats prioritizes network security at all levels of our business. As such, we conform to the guidelines established by National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST). Our security measures include firewall provisioning and intrusion detection.

Data Transmission: Exchange of data between Stamats and its clients and partners takes place only via secure means (HTTPs or FTPS).

Data Storage: Data that Stamats receives from its clients is owned by its clients. Data is stored, processed, transferred, and maintained exclusively on target servers (as opposed to portable devices).

Encryption: Stamats adheres to the Advanced Encryption Standard (AES). Backup/recovery data is stored in encrypted form (no less than 128-bit key).

Return/Destruction of Data: Upon fulfillment or termination of contracted services, Stamats shall return or destroy all client data received or collected (e.g., through research or analytic activities). Transfer/destruction will be confirmed in writing within 30 days of termination services (or earlier, if required by the client).

Backups: We perform nightly backups.

question: Achieving Success with Technology
answer:
Please review the following specific qualifications regarding the quality of our approach, services, reporting, and results.

Achieving Success with TechnologyAs a Google and Facebook Marketing Partner, Stamats has access to exclusive beta opportunities, brand ambassadors, and vertical experts to ensure our campaigns are set up for success and our clients stay ahead of the curve in the constantly evolving world of paid digital advertising.

Our team invests in training time every single week to stay abreast of trends and best practices for our clients. As part of our certification, we receive additional support and access to beta products before the standard Google Ads user. For example, Stamats was one of the first to have access to Performance Max to test for lead generation.

Stamats also uses the industry-leading DSP, Basis Technologies DSP, to target the right audience, in the right place, at the right time. Using myriad channels such as social, animated display, native, digital audio, digital video, and Connected TV, Basis Technologies has proven to generate the greatest return on our clients’ investments in digital media.

Optimizing for Best Results

Digital Campaign Building and Execution

From day one, you will partner with a dedicated team that is ready to go to work. While we know there is a lot of collaboration that goes into sharing access to digital platforms, setting up new user accounts, performing keyword analysis, and more–Stamats will make everything as easy as possible for you, so you can focus on what truly matters and not sweat the small things.

Using pre-built, easy-to-complete Discovery documents and Basecamp for file sharing and communication, our teams will quickly build a solid foundation for the campaign. Stamats also uses a variety of digital tools to maximize campaign performance. From the ad platforms themselves to proprietary scripts for automation, Stamats knows the right tactics and tools for any marketing task.

Working with Stamats means working with a digital marketing team focused on closely managing ad spend across channels to maximize goal conversions. Our performance reports not only provide common data such as clicks and conversions, Stamats provides the actionable insights that keep campaigns moving forward.

Stamats’ digital marketers optimize campaigns every day because ‘fire and forget’ seldom leads to success. Google rewards campaigns that are smartly optimized with lower cost per click, better ad placement, and more ad impressions per dollar. Stamats campaigns tend to earn more leads for the same dollar investment year over year.

Ongoing campaign optimization is central to the Stamats approach to SEM campaign management. Another key is not simply enabling Google’s AI-based automations but managing its performance. Google’s AI automations are available to everyone, and while most will enable the features few will actively work to provide the algorithms with the added data they need to outperform average.

Testing Reduces Click Costs and Increases Conversions

Our team has a culture of continually testing. Utilizing segmentation, holdout groups, and other measurement techniques, we are constantly testing for improved performance. This applies not just to ads and keywords but to landing page copy and imagery too. At the end of each test, we provide a transparent picture of the outcome and offer strategies to improve performance and exceed campaign goals.

For all campaigns, we also review landing pages to identify any bottlenecks or gaps in the desired conversion and micro-conversion process. Our perspective comes from years of experience. While ads attract interested prospects and send them to the site, it’s the landing page that must do the heavy lifting and convert interest into action.

In an example from a previous campaign, the Stamats team was able to improve the click-through rate (CTR) significantly month-to-month through increasingly optimized ads and an improved landing page experience. This improved performance led to a 50% reduction in ad costs and breakeven after just two months! All this while maintaining or exceeding the planned conversion goals.

Overall Campaign Click-Through Rate

Month 2: Stamats Management

Month 3: Stamats Management

Month 4: Stamats Management

10.65%

Testing Ad Creative Makes a Difference

While social campaigns usually provide greater conversion rates compared to display, both channels equally benefit from catchy, intriguing, and engaging creative–and we will test to maximize performance (e.g., In the YouTube video ad sequence shown on the right, swapping the order of videos lead to a 9.52% increase in impressions for the second video in the series).

question: Platform Expertise
answer:
1. Google Ads: including Search, Display, Performance Max, Demand Gen, Retargeting, YouTube

Stamats is a Google Partner with specializations in Search Advertising and individual specializations in Google Analytics. The Search Advertising certification demonstrates proficiency in search advertising, including best practices for creating, managing, and optimizing Google Ad search campaigns.

2. Meta: Facebook/Instagram, 3. LinkedIn, 4. TikTok, 6. Snapchat

As a Meta Business Partner, Stamats is certified in Meta (Facebook) advertising based on our expertise in helping clients generate the best campaign results. Our team also specializes in LinkedIn advertising, and we are AdTech Academy by Basis Technologies Certified and Meta Certified Creative Strategy Professionals.

Each social media platform has its own set of unique users. Facebook reaches parents, the number one influencer of high school students and their choice of college. Instagram, Tik Tok, Snapchat, and Twitch can engage higher education’s youngest audiences. LinkedIn is terrific to use when promoting graduate courses.

Did you know that when a college makes an organic post on Facebook/Instagram less than 30% of its followers get alerted to the new post? Paid ads on Facebook are the surest way to reach the bulk of your brand followers on the platform.

Social media platforms like Snapchat, TikTok, and Instagram increasingly provide academic brands opportunities to engage with younger audiences by capturing their attention with visually appealing creative and messaging. The integration of strategic social media content can have a synergistic effect by increasing awareness and share of voice.

Strategic social media content can deliver a boost in site traffic over time by encouraging engagement between targeted audiences and relevant content.

5. Twitter/X

While we have served ads on Twitter in the past, no current clients have used the platform in the last two to three years. Limited ad reach and frequency, plus content moderation concerns have made the platform less attractive to our higher ed client base.

7. Hulu & 10. OTT

Over-the-top (OTT) video has reshaped advertising in significant ways. Hulu is now part of the Disney media group where we also have access to ESPN, Marvel as an ad platform. OTT advertising provides a significant opportunity to reach younger audiences that advertisers are unable to target through other digital media.

Our clients have had success using OTT at the top of the funnel, using messages that promote branded point-of-difference to cut through the noise.

Beyond the OTT direct buys through Hulu/Disney, Stamats uses programmatic placement to reach additional channels and sources. Programmatic OTT is a lower cost option that offers less specific targeting. Because programmatic placement is less specific and often less relevant, we favor using the Hulu/Disney option when affordable.

8. Spotify & 9. Pandora

Internet radio plays a similar role to OTT video in maintaining top of funnel awareness, but the media can also be very effective in promoting time-based events. Using internet radio, Stamats has helped clients exceed engagement expectations for open house events and campus tours.

One word of caution—unlike OTT, internet radio subscribers get an ad-free experience, so reach into the platform’s audience may be limited.

11. Display (programmatic)

Display advertising with banner ads, video ads, or audios is the #1 tool for market reach. Display is also closely tied to top- and mid-funnel awareness building with an affordable cost-per-ad impression. While technically all display ad platforms are ‘programmatic’, Stamats uses Basis to serve display ads using curated and purchased lists. If brand safety and placement accountability are important, the Google and Microsoft Ads platforms are the best options.

Display ads have also been the format used for remarketing to site visitors and for targeting and purchased audience lists. However, display advertising is headed into a period of change. With strict internet privacy laws going into effect across the country, remarketing and audience-based ad serving reaches far fewer prospects than they once did.

In fact, these two powerful tactics already struggle to reach users on the Safari or Firefox browsers, and for most sites, those browsers represent half or more of all site visits.

Stamats works with clients to develop their own first-party audiences for advertising, an option that can fill the need for remarketing-like follow-up advertising. Stamats also works to help set accurate expectations of what Display ads can do, and what they don’t do as well.

12. Geofencing/Geotargeting

Geofencing allows us to target users by where they are in the moment—or where they have been in the past. Powerful geofencing capabilities zoom in on granular points on a map, perhaps a specific building or campus. An example is serving ads with discounts to anyone who enters the campus bookstore. Venue Replay is an extension of geofencing that looks backward in time to serve ads to people who were at a specific location at a specific time. For example, we could target prospective students who were at a college fair three months ago.

Others: Specify

YouTube is the second most used search engine in the world. As such it provides a high degree of targetability, and many opportunities for lower cost engagement, reaching prospects around the clock with long- and short-form video messages.

Video Sequencing—A tactic within the YouTube platform, video sequencing is a multi-touch campaign type that serves video ads in a prescribed order. A popular format is ‘Introduce & Reinforce,’ a long video ad is first shown to the prospect to introduce the offer, followed by a series of short supporting video ads over time.

Bumper Ads—Short, six-second ads that show at the beginning of YouTube videos. Used to boost awareness of special offers, events, and brand, bumpers are also used in remarketing campaigns. Low cost and “unskippable,” video bumper ads can help any advertiser remain top of mind between site visits.

question: Campaign Reporting
answer:
Describe the methodology that would be used to track and evaluate the marketing plan’s effectiveness. Include KPIs (Key Performance Indicator) and steps you would take if the plan is not delivering the expected results.

Stamats Dashboards provide real-time insights into an institution’s digital performance without a need for technical knowledge or access to Google Analytics (GA). Using Google Looker Studio, the Stamats Google-certified team will plan a custom dashboard that suits [Client Name]'s specialized needs. The real-time 24/7 campaign digital dashboard will be developed so that your team has quick and easy access to ad performance metrics.

We adhere to the best practice of keeping KPIs focused to a select number of metrics to avoid data overload and “analysis paralysis” to move forward. From those key points, we include additional metrics that may enhance the view of those KPIs. Broadly, KPIs are mapped by objective:

Awareness

Consideration

Cost/quality visit (often with a time-on-site or action requirement)

Lead generation

Cost/lead (with a minimum volume threshold)

Conversion rate (rate of visits that convert to lead)

For lead gen measurement, it is crucial to understand contributors to the results:

Cost/Lead (Media-Creative-Audience)

Conversion Rate (Landing Page Effectiveness, Media-Creative-Audience)

Device behavior can also play a role, so we employ heatmaps and carry out secret shopper/user experience audits and click tracking videos

Additional metrics include time on site, pages consumed, and bounce rate, but it is important to remember that these metrics are based exclusively on click visits (less than 1% of the buy in most cases) and depend heavily on landing page effectiveness and design—even load time. Measurement should focus beyond clicks to assess the effectiveness of all channels working together.

We use Google Looker Studio dashboards to import metrics in real time for campaigns, and we’ll talk through them with you at our regularly scheduled meetings (or as questions arise!). This allows for campaign managers to make data-based decisions for optimization on a 24/7 basis and enables clients to view their campaigns 24/7. The dashboard imports data via open API with most platforms. It is customizable to address various campaign builds and objectives/KPIs. Beyond dashboards, clients also receive more thorough monthly reports with key insights and narrative from Stamats digital strategists.

question: Conflict of Interest Policy
answer:
Stamats has worked with colleges and universities for more than 70 years. During that time, we have worked with clients in overlapping markets and have never had any issues with conflicts of interest. We maintain separate and discrete relationships with each client and promise to guard the details of our work together to ensure our mutual success is a foundational part of our business. Far from just a simple policy, discretion and professionalism are part of Stamats’ culture at every level.

question: Stamats Understanding of the Needs of the College
answer:
Given your array of audiences and significant online offerings, the [Client Name] website’s first impression is of utmost importance. Local, regional, and even national (you mention SNHU) institutions offer stiff competition and often substantial budgets. Your site is competing against colleges and universities with aggressive ad campaigns and effective landing pages. Therefore, [Client Name] must “sell” to prospective students with the very first impression. We know your new site must:

Answer the questions that users have as well as their original search intent

Have a clear call-to-action design approach.

Present intuitive, easy-to-scan, streamlined (sometimes that means fewer pages!) user journeys for the wide array of student populations interested in [Client Name].

Be optimized for SEO performance.

Be able to clearly track funnel engagement.

Offer personalization for custom content based on user behaviors, user location, user device, and visit history. By creating targeted messages to audiences, similar to Amazon and retail promotions, [Client Name] can better support a nurture strategy and optimize the real estate of your site…and get the conversions you need.

Through it all, your site must reflect your brand, your principles, your value, and the [Client Name] experience (and demonstrate why the college is the best choice for students of all types).

Sites must support brand in content tone, design, typeface, and imagery. Your new website should feel like the experience the student will have in their program.

And finally, we will build a site planned for your future. Our approach is to create extendable sites that are easy to maintain across campus—not only the main site but also for individual departments, centers, units, etc. We do this because our team members have been product owners and site webmasters themselves and understand the importance of sustainability. We love to see our clients utilize the customized and flexible templates and components we’ve developed not only for updates but for new initiatives as they arise.

question: Company History - Long Version
answer:
The History of Stamats Communications, Inc. - Long Version
Our Founding
Stamats Communications, Inc. traces its roots back to 1923 when it was founded as the French-Stamats Company by Frank French and Herbert Stamats. Originally a promotion and advertising agency catering to local retailers, their enterprise quickly expanded to serve stores throughout the Midwest by providing turnkey or syndicated marketing programs.
The company expanded again in 1934 with the launch of Stamats’ first national magazine. After Frank French’s death in 1934, Herbert Stamats purchased French’s interest in the company and renamed it Stamats Publishing Company.
Early Evolution: 1940s-1960s
As its publishing business grew, so did the company’s need for high-quality printing. To meet demand and control quality, Stamats launched a state-of-the-art printing operation in the 1940s. This dimension of the business drew new customers who were in need of commercial printing services.
The company expanded into business-to-business media in 1942 by acquiring the Porter-Langtry Company of Chicago and along with it, BUILDINGS magazine. BUILDINGS’ circulation grew dramatically in the 1950s and soon became the leading national publication serving building owners and managers.
In the 1950s, Stamats began offering design and writing services for custom publications. Early clients included a few college and universities in need of high-quality brochures and catalogs for their admissions departments. By providing a packaged approach that included writing, photography, design, and printing, Stamats’ reputation as a “one-stop shop” for first-rate higher education material quickly spread.
With the passing of Herbert Stamats in 1966, company leadership changed once again. Herbert’s son-in-law, H. G. “Fed” Hedges, and son, Peter O. Stamats, assumed management of the company as president and executive vice president, respectively.
Steady Expansion: 1970s and ‘80s
With its growing custom publication business, the company expanded its facilities in 1974 with the addition of a new printing plant.
In 1981, the company’s growth into business lines beyond printing was marked by a name change: Stamats Communications, Inc. reflected a broader business focus and allowed the company to adapt to a more dynamic marketplace.
Throughout 1983, Stamats leadership continued to evolve. That year, Guy Wendler joined the team as president of a joint venture with a Fortune 500 company. In 1987, Wendler was named vice president. Peter O. Stamats became president of Stamats Communications, Inc. in 1983 and Fed Hedges assumed the role of chairman.
The company expanded into the trade show business with the successful launch of The Buildings Show held in St. Louis, Missouri in 1984. With rapid growth in attendance, subsequent shows were presented in Philadelphia; Washington, D.C.; Houston; and Atlanta. In 2006, The Buildings Show merged with the BOMA Conference and Expo in a 50/50 partnership with the Buildings Owners and Managers Association International.
In 1984, Stamats produced and released a special interest video on the Grand Canyon National Park, the first in its American Visions series on America’s national parks. Tom Hedges, one of the founder’s grandsons, led this innovative line of business.
By 1988, the company had grown to be the leading provider of custom publications to colleges and universities in the nation. As colleges expanded their own marketing efforts, Stamats responded with additional capabilities, adding market research and consulting to its mix of services. This business line was designated internally as the Educational Services Group (ESG).
Another of the founder’s grandsons, Peter S. Stamats, joined the company in 1988. After many successful years in management at Hewlett-Packard, Peter launched a new phase of his career, becoming Stamats’ production director.
Focused Innovation: 1990s-Early 2000s
As the Education Services Group expanded with account executives, consultants, writers, and designers, the company made the tactical decision to outsource its printing services. Stamats sold its commercial printing operations to a local company, Cedar Graphics, in 1990.
That same year, the company made another important acquisition when it purchased a San Francisco-based business publication serving the California meetings market. With this move, Stamats established its San Francisco office.
Stamats’ reputation was further enhanced when Dr. Robert Sevier started writing and speaking about integrated marketing in higher education in the early 1990s. His thought leadership and promotion skills were instrumental in positioning Stamats as a vocal advocate for higher education and an industry innovator.
Guy Wendler was named president 1995, succeeding Peter O. Stamats who became chairman. During this period, Stamats’ board of directors was expanded to include outside directors.
1995 also marked the start of an important expansion in the meetings market with Meetings California covering not only California, but 12 additional western states. To reflect this broader reach, the brand was renamed Meetings West.
Stamats has always been an early adopter of technology and in 1996, launched a suite of new web sites including www.buildings.com, www.stamats.com and www.meetingsweb.com. At the same time, the company began providing interactive media services to colleges and universities across the nation.
Stamats’ headquarters facility in Cedar Rapids, Iowa was remodeled in 1998 and the street address changed to 615 5th Street SE. Bill Stamats, another grandson of the founder, joined the company in the same year after working for two other family-owned businesses in Cedar Rapids—Highway Equipment Company (now New Leader Manufacturing) and J.E. Adams Industries.
Another brand was added to the Stamats portfolio of business publications with the launch of Meetings South in 1999. In 2000, Meetings East was launched—a move that gave Stamats national market coverage in the meetings industry. That same year, the company’s circulation department was renamed Database Marketing Services, reflecting the expansion of its audience development efforts into multiple media channels including print, online, and in-person. In 2000, gross sales for the company surpassed the $20 million mark.
In the first year of the new millennium, Peter S. Stamats was named executive vice president and CFO and Bill Stamats took the reins of the Educational Services Group—a set of coordinated moves that would position the company for further strategic growth.
In 2003, Stamats focused on its historic headquarters, completing renovations to the parking lot and outdoor event area. The enhancements created a welcoming, campus-like atmosphere for staff, clients, and visitors.
Stamats combined its business-to-business media operations into one unit in 2004 and dubbed it Stamats Business Media. That same year, the company launched Meetings MidAmerica as a spin-off of Meetings East. Later that year, Stamats Business Media partnered with VerticalXchange to launch BuildingsXchange, a face-to-face buyer/seller event for the commercial buildings industry.
The company acquired Interiors & Sources® in 2004 as a sister brand to BUILDINGS. Interiors & Sources® expanded the company’s coverage of the commercial buildings industry into commercial interior design. This unit of Stamats Business Media was then renamed the Commercial Buildings Group.
The Great Iowa Flood of 2008
In June of 2008, catastrophic flooding of most of the rivers in Eastern Iowa resulted in $6 billion in damages statewide. Cedar Rapids was particularly hard hit and Stamats’ headquarters suffered major damage as water from the Cedar River crested 19 feet above flood stage.
When it became clear that Stamats’ headquarters was threatened, many employees voluntarily navigated there by boat and worked tirelessly to salvage Stamats’ vast collection of artwork, important files, and essential equipment—funneling the most crucial items to lofted offices and the building’s rooftop. This humbling show of solidarity speaks to the culture and people that have contributed to the company’s 100 years of success.
After the flood, staff members relocated to temporary space or home offices during the many months of reconstruction. Stamats completed the rebuilding of its facility to LEED® standards in 2009 and all employees who had been displaced by the Great Iowa Flood of 2008 returned to the newly-renovated offices. That same year, the company entered the social media business and began providing expanded digital development services in areas such as SEO and SEM.
2013-2023: The Digital Shift
Stamats is no stranger to adapting to changing trends. The last decade has brought many twists and turns both internally and externally from strategic business decisions and digital transformations to pivoting in a global pandemic.
One thing remains—Stamats’ dedication to providing impactful, creative services to its clients no matter the platform.
Making the Right Moves
Traditional print outreach and advertising were declining, and the world was shifting to new and emerging digital communication technology. Not only were the communication platforms shifting but now jobs and responsibilities had to morph.
Stamats looked for new methods to better serve colleges and universities. In an effort to stay ahead of the curve, we underwent a significant digital transformation.
“We went from being a print-centric company with a lot of the products and offerings in the markets that we served to becoming digitally centric,” said Stamats President and CEO, Peter Stamats. “We found ourselves transitioning our staff and retooling.”
Stamats has found ways and opportunities to expand its reach to help connect more companies to their audiences. We launched Audativ, the audience management services, and tools to help other publishers, as well as branching out into the healthcare industry.
Staying Nimble Through the Unknown
Towards the end of the decade, Stamats had to navigate a different kind of transformation–one that no one saw coming and disrupted the whole world and how we operate. COVID-19.
But working in disparate teams was not new news for us; Stamats was well-versed in the remote, results-focused working style. The president himself had been working remotely for years before the pandemic. “The pandemic proved to all of us that we can work remotely. And that we kind of like it,” said Stamats.
Trends and technology will continue to change but you can expect Stamats to hold true to its founding values of providing high-quality, creative services to our customers for the next 100 years.
New Century...New Home Base
After 89 years at its Fifth Street SE location, Stamats moved its corporate headquarters to 308 Third Street SE, Suite 200, effective November 1, 2023.
During the 2020 pandemic, Stamats established a flexible work environment policy, allowing employees to choose where they best meet the needs of their clients. As a result, Stamats has almost 80 employees across the U.S., some working in home offices, and others coming into the Cedar Rapids headquarters.
“With fewer employees needing a corporate office, we could better use our resources in a smaller office space,” said Stamats President Peter S. Stamats. “We will miss the history of our former space, but we are committed to the Cedar Rapids community and look forward to being in the heart of downtown.”

question: Stamats FERPA Compliance Information Technology Policy
answer:
FERPA laws and regulations contain many areas that are not clearly defined. Much of the expectation is that all parties involved take reasonable actions to prevent unauthorized access to data. As part of the compliance process clients often provide a contract outlining specific action items. Stamats will follow client policy/direction and comply with these specific actionable items. In addition to client requirements, Stamats follows the confidential data handling policy.

Confidential personally identifiable data handling policy:

Perform security measures to protect personal data from non-authorized sources.

Use data exclusively for intended purpose.

Destroy all client data containing sensitive data once delivered to the client.

Use encryption technologies when transferring data between parties.

Require vendors to follow confidential data handling policy.

File transfer procedure:

Use secure client transfer system where applicable.

Require 256-bit encryption when sending electronically.

Expire/Destroy data availability once transaction is complete.

We do not anticipate accessing HIPAA-protected information over the course of this relationship. Should circumstances change, we will follow required HIPAA regulations.

question: Policies and Procedures in Place to Maintain Compliance with HIPPA
answer:
We do not have any specific policy around HIPPA. The following is our Employee Privacy Policy.

In this age of the Internet where privacy has become an increasing concern, we take your privacy very seriously. The privacy and security of your personal data (“Personal Information”) which we collect from you is important to us. It is equally important that you understand how we handle this data. Stamats will not knowingly collect or use Personal Information in any manner not consistent with this policy, as it may be amended from time to time, and applicable laws. Collection of Information In the course of conducting our business and complying with federal, state, and local government regulations governing such matters as employment, tax, insurance, etc., we must collect Personal Information from you. The nature of the information collected varies somewhat for each employee, depending on your employment responsibilities, the location of the facility where you work, and other factors. We collect Personal Information from you solely for business purposes, including those related directly to your employment with Stamats, and those required by governmental agencies.

Use of the Information Collected

The primary purposes for collection, storage and/or use of your Personal Information include, but are not limited to:

Human Resources Management. We collect, store, analyze, and share (internally) Personal Information in order to attract, retain, and motivate a highly qualified workforce. This includes recruiting, compensation planning, succession planning, reorganization needs, performance assessment, training, employee benefit administration, compliance with applicable legal requirements, and communication with employees and/or their representatives.

Business Processes and Management. Personal Information is used to run our business operations including, for example, scheduling work assignments, managing Stamats assets, reporting and/or releasing public data (e.g., annual reports, etc.), and populating employee directories. Information may also be used to comply with government regulation.

Safety and Security Management. We use such Personal Information as appropriate to ensure the safety and protection of employees, assets, resources, and communities.

Communication and Identification. We use your Personal Information to identify you and to communicate with you. Limited Disclosure Stamats acts to protect your Personal Information and ensure that unauthorized individuals do not have access to such information by using security measures to protect Personal Information.

We will not knowingly disclose, sell, or otherwise distribute your Personal Information to any third party without your knowledge and, where appropriate, your express written permission, except where disclosure is reasonably necessary to comply with the law. Security of Personal Information We employ reasonable security measures and technologies, such as password protection, encryption, physical locks, etc., to protect the confidentiality of your Personal Information. Only authorized employees have access to Personal Information. If you are an employee with such authorization it is imperative that you take the appropriate safeguards to protect such information. Paper and other hard copy containing Personal Information (or any other confidential information) should be secured in a locked location when not in use. Computers and other access points should be secured when not in use by logging out or locking. Passwords and user IDs should be guarded and not shared. When no longer necessary for business purposes, paper and hard copies should be immediately destroyed using paper shredders or similar devices. Do not leave copies in unsecured locations waiting to be shredded or otherwise destroyed. Do not make or distribute unauthorized copies of documents or other tangible mediums containing Personal Information. Electronic files containing Personal Information should only be stored on secure computers and not copied or otherwise shared with unauthorized individuals within or outside of Stamats.

Stamats will make reasonable efforts to secure Personal Information stored or transmitted electronically from hackers or other persons who are not authorized to access such information. Any violation or potential violation of this policy should be reported to your immediate supervisor, designated manager, or Human Resources. The failure by any employee to follow these privacy policies may result in discipline up to and including discharge of the employee. Any questions or suggestions regarding this policy may also be directed to your immediate supervisor, designated manager, or Human Resources.

question: Responsible financial stewardship
answer:
With over 100 years of experience, we understand that the best strategies and executions are only effective when paired with responsible financial stewardship. From the initial pricing in our proposal to ongoing project work, we treat your budget as if it were our own, providing clear cost estimates upfront and delivering projects efficiently.

Stamats typically works on a project- and fee-based model, though we can also provide hourly rates. Our fees cover account leadership, professional services, and project management. Travel expenses are billed separately with prior written client approval.

We ensure transparency in project scope development, collaborating closely with your team to define goals, timelines, and parameters. This open process guides our planning tools and decisions.

To keep hourly rates and fees among the most competitive in the market, we carefully monitor industry fee/rate standards and trends among firms whose offerings and capabilities mirror ours. This is done by critically reviewing other agencies’ proposals submitted in response to RFPs issued by public institutions via FOIA and/or “sunshine” requests.

More specifically, we will be particularly attentive to:

Clear communication regarding budget status, projections, and risks

Detailed documentation for invoices, receipts, and other relevant documents

Regular/ongoing reviews to discuss status and any potential issues

Approval process through project managers and finance teams for any budget adjustments

Internal feedback and discussion with recommended risk mitigation

question: Data Expectations
answer:
Funnel data for undergraduate student inquiry, admitted, accepted records over the last three to five years provided in Excel; this will serve as definition for the recruitment footprint.

Funnel data for graduate student inquiry, admitted, accepted records over the last three to five years provided in Excel; this will serve as definition for the recruitment footprint.

Contact list for every research survey proposed. Note that adequate telephone records must be supplied for each audience to achieve the sample sizes indicated. While telematching records without telephone may supplement the list to dial upon, in some cases, clients may desire to purchase additional prospect records to supplement the viable lists of records with telephone numbers. Additional list purchases are the responsibility of the university.

Written confirmations of approved survey instruments by main project contact at the university.

We also have the following suggestions for lists for telephone research:

Our preferred format for the contact list is Excel.

We ask that you supply all records for a given population, rather than just a portion of them. Most studies are analyzed using statistics that assume the sample follows random or true probability sampling procedures. This means every member of the population of interest needs to have an equal chance of participation. If we have access to only a portion of all the names, this assumption no longer holds. We ask that even records without telephone numbers be supplied, even if they are unlikely to ever be contacted. This allows us to compare the study sample data with the overall population and understand what differences, if any, exist between the two.

In terms of the information to be provided about each student/record, more is always better. Every potential data point (address, age, gender, ethnicity, test scores, intended major, visit activity, major/program of interest, zip code, etc.) may be valuable when attempting to profile interactions between perceptions and demographics. Please indicate with each record the start term, and please indicate if they applied or not.

We highly recommend including a unique ID number or record identifier for each name, just in case there is a desire to connect each record with other data you might have.

Please do not include social security numbers, personal financial data, or health information with the records.

Each population should be supplied in a separate spreadsheet or on a separate worksheet. The populations of interest for this study include:

Prospective, inquiring, accepted, admitted undergraduate students—telephone survey, needs telephone numbers

Prospective, inquiring, accepted, admitted parents of undergraduate students—telephone survey, needs telephone numbers

Inquiring, accepted, admitted transfer students—telephone survey, needs telephone numbers

Inquiring, accepted, admitted graduate students—telephone survey, needs telephone numbers

Guidance counselors—web survey, needs emails

Alumni of [Client Name]—web survey, needs emails

Community or business leaders—telephone survey, needs telephone numbers

question: Call-to-Action Strategy: Intentional, Action-Driven Content
answer:
Creating and using a call-to-action (CTA) strategy can improve the user experience and simplify and improve conversion on your blog, landing page, or website. A more thoughtful CTA approach, driven by data and audience needs, can help you meet business goals.

Personalizing calls-to-action (CTAs) tailored to the user experience can increase conversion rates by more than 200% according to HubSpot.

In this interactive session, you’ll learn the uses and benefits of several types of CTAs and how to implement your strategy simply and effectively in your content. Creating effective CTAs is an art form, particularly in content marketing (blogs and social media). You don’t want to sound “salesy,” and you don’t want to be too vague. You must understand your audience and intuit their next steps to position your content and CTAs in relevant, proper pathways.

Here’s a sneak peek: The overall goal of a CTA strategy is to make it easy for your site visitors to access your services with as few action steps as possible. The best CTA strategies share six key traits:

Audience-centric: Focus on what’s most important to your audience.

Built on digital best practices: These include using color elements that are right for screen readers and using specific, action-oriented words.

Data-driven: Use A/B testing to track what’s working and not working and adjust on the fly.

Designed with relevant user journey story pathways: Consider what makes the most sense for the reader, from where their eyes should go on a page to what their next step should be when they leave the page.

Educational and entertaining: You don’t have to be witty but use a friendly and personable tone.

Infused with media and text elements: CTAs should stand out but align with the design and flow of the page.

Create CTA pathways that serve this give-and-take relationship and you will naturally increase your audience’s loyalty, transforming visitors into cheerleaders for your brand, resulting in increased brand recognition and sales.

question: Approach to Link Building Strategy and Implementation
answer:
Developing backlinks to boost and hold organic ranking is playing the long game; it’s an investment of effort that pays off over time. Quality backlinks directly contribute to organic search ranking, yet most web pages found in search results have no backlinks. This information helps us define the scope of our effort; the job isn’t to earn every backlink—it’s to earn the right backlinks.

We begin by assessing your site’s most important pages versus your competitors’ pages, which helps us learn where we need to prioritize backlink building.

For example, the [Client Name] SAMPLE PROGRAM PAGE has backlinks on XX linking domains, while the same content on the COMPETITOR SAME SAMPLE PROGRAM PAGE has XX linking domains. Backlink building won’t be necessary to beat out COMPETITOR in search results. Still further, the content page for SAMPLE PROGRAM PAGE from ONLINE COMPETITOR has XXX domains contributing backlinks, most from related news sites.

Earning backlinks isn’t a quick process, but there are ways to kickstart the initiative. For one, take a page from the University of Phoenix playbook and post PR-like content marketing stories to relevant news sites that lead readers to the content on your own site.

Stamats will work with [Client Name]to identify the most important content needing a lift in organic search ranking. Then, after assessing top search competitors, Stamats will develop a plan for backlink building that can be executed by either [Client Name]or Stamats.

question: Approach to Optimization
answer:
First, we follow an agile methodology—taking small, strategic steps to learn quickly, adapt fast, and keep your project moving. This allows for flexibility, faster testing, and adjustments based on audience behavior, KPIs, and shifting priorities throughout the year. Our goal is to keep you informed and aligned as we refine messaging and tactics to meet your goals.

Second, in SEO, we focus on task prioritization. With so many potential tactics, we identify which actions will deliver the greatest impact first. Clients consistently cite our SEO prioritization map and results tracking as key to ensuring their efforts are both strategic and effective.

question: Three-Part SEO Audit

Pages missing from the sitemap

question: Describe the ways in which the CMS facilitates and enhances SEO.
answer:
Most CMS platforms today offer solid SEO support, including control over titles, tags, meta descriptions, and keywords. We recommend using clean, search-friendly URLs without dynamic parameters (e.g., www.schoolname.edu/admissions/), which most platforms allow.

Stamats also provides specific SEO guidance, addressing issues like duplicate or missing titles, poorly written descriptions, and other key on-page elements to strengthen your site’s search performance.

question: How would you mitigate the impact to SEO of moving to a new platform, especially considering that the new site will likely have 90% fewer pages than the current site, broken links, etc.?
answer:
Contrary to what you may expect, modern search engines may reward you for cutting pages. Quality content and page relationships matter more than quantity. A small, well-crafted user journey will outperform a sprawling site–with both users and the search algorithms. To earn a high site rating with Google:

Structure your site carefully

Curate outbound links

Optimize individual pages

More and more, the search engines reward better user experiences.

question: Approach to Competitor Analysis
answer:
We will conduct a comprehensive Competitor Analysis of [Client Name] and TOP COMPETITOR, plus up to two additional competitors. Our findings from this analysis will inform:

What keywords competitors are winning organically and through paid campaigns

Any naming changes needed for labels

Realistic expectations for surpassing competitors

Best practices to gain a competitive advantage

Baseline SEO performance of your competitors and your site for comparison after the project

We understand the value of ranking high for desired search queries—and with 70 years of experience serving high ed clients, we know the landscape is competitive. As we work to craft the best solution for you, we’ll be guided by answers to the following SEO-related questions:

What words do prospective students use to search? Are those queries answered on [Client Name]’s site?

What non-academic sites are “stealing” your SERP spots? What word swaps can mitigate that?

Is the content pathway clear and navigable once the user arrives on the site?

How will keyword choice and placement improve SEO?

How does site architecture support content needs and considerations?

How does site architecture promote site and page indexing and ranking?

How often will content be refreshed?

How will the linking structure promote genuine and favorable rankings?

Stamats uses two key platforms to conduct keyword research that provide valuable insights into clients’ current SEO landscape: Moz Pro and SpyFu.

These tools guide our team on which keywords [Client Name] already ranks for, which pages are top-ranking, which are on the cusp, how you stack up against competitors, and which keywords to focus on first. We also use these tools to discover new keywords that could reach Google Page 1, increase impressions, and draw organic website traffic.

On a practical level, we recommend meaningful and search engine-friendly URLs, with no dynamic parameters or cryptic file names (for example, www.schoolname.edu/admissions/). Most platforms provide clients with complete control over the filenames, allowing the site to have user- and SEO-friendly URLs. Importantly, Stamats provides prescriptive counsel regarding SEO recommendations on duplicate titles, missing titles, long titles, short titles, duplicate descriptions, and more.

We also can provide training to your content providers. Our SEO 101 Workshop will help you learn the basics: What SEO is, why it is important, and how to implement best practices. Upon completion, you will have the foundation to make your content rank higher in Google searches and attract relevant engagements. Our proven approach provides the tools to create clear, easy-to-find content for your website and improve your search engine optimization (SEO). Tips we’ll cover include:

Google’s Helpful Content Update: guiding users to information they seek

Appropriately adding relevant search phrases to your content

Optimizing your content for search engines and for users

Using links from other relevant websites in your content

Monitoring and measuring your search rankings and results

Deliverable: Competitor analysis with recommendations for back-end and front-end tactics to improve your ranking quickly and over time.

question: Quantitative Research Steps - Detailed
answer:
Select the Research Sample

Stamats requests the contact list in Excel and can provide guidance on data fields that are useful to include. Note that since this is an email invitation survey, the number of viable and active email records available at the time of the study will play a large role in the sample size that can be achieved. Once Stamats has reviewd the available sample, we provide a more detailed sampling plan with realistic expectation for responses based on the composition of the pool and representation of key sub-populations.

Develop the Survey Instruments

The survey—developed with your crucial input—will include structured questions (e.g., scaled responses, dichotomous responses, multiple choice answers, etc.) and unstructured answers (unaided, allowing respondents to type in their answers). Demographic and behavioral data is also captured to allow data segmentation and understand important differences between segments of the respondent pool(s).

Conduct Fieldwork

Stamats will administer all surveys. During fieldwork, Stamats will monitor response rates and data-collection procedures. To bolster response rates, Stamats will offer an incentive for external audiences and send up to two follow-up/reminder emails. We also manage all unsubscribes and purge all invalid emails.

Analyze Data

Following fieldwork, our research analysts will use a variety of statistical techniques to ensure that all valuable findings are extracted from the data. Statistical techniques applied to similar projects include:

Analysis of variance and difference of means tests

Maximum Difference Scaling to determine best/worst college selection factors and defining [Client Name] characteristics

Scatter plot of brand attributes to map importance of college selection criteria to perceptions of [Client Name]characteristics

Audience segmentation to identify clarifying messaging and perspectives across a diverse array of respondents

Audience segmentation may include, but is not limited to, respondent type (e.g., current student, prospective student, parent, faculty, staff, alumni, donor, etc.), and demographics (e.g., male vs. female, geographic location, etc.).

question: Secret Shopper
answer:
A secret shopper experience can also be added to the detailed analysis of specific academic programs. An online inquiry form for a specific program (e.g., BS Biology) is completed at your institution and four others. Outreach attempts and messages from competitors are compared to your institution. Third-party observations in the form of secret shopper inquiries bring insight regarding;

Customer experience with initial contact (tone, timeliness, amount of effort required by student)

Frequency of engagement with the student

Channels used to reach the student

Messages used to attract students

As [Client Name] is also included in this exercise, outcomes may point to areas of improvement to consider in the early communication experience. The secret shopper observation is activated at the same time of day for your brand and the four competitors and outreach tactics are recorded over a 14-day window.

Stamats to run an inventory of current site and up to 10 identified subsites

question: Brand Training for Employees
answer:
We integrate education and training into every client relationship, recognizing the importance of engaging organizational stakeholders in marketing efforts. Through data, testing, workshops, and ongoing engagement, we help guide institutional leaders to understand the value of a unified brand.

At Stamats, we believe marketing is a shared responsibility, not just for the marketing or advancement teams. A brand is best brought to life by those who interact daily with key audiences.

Our extensive experience with brand clients shows that [Client Name] will benefit greatly from broad internal buy-in. We often see untapped potential in institutional stakeholders, and by fostering internal engagement and executing meaningful launches, we can significantly amplify [Client Name]'s marketing impact.

We recommend the project team take into consideration the following:

Campaign ownership is the first step in animating and rallying the passions of internal stakeholders around the brand.

Internal engagement enriches the creative process.

A strong internal launch enlightens, informs, and inspires internal stakeholders and converts them into passionate brand ambassadors.

An inspirational internal launch fuels collective pride and a shared sense of purpose.

Brand-Related Training: Living the Brand Workshops

Stamats views brand marketing as a shared responsibility, not just for marketing, communications, admissions, or advancement. A brand thrives when those who interact with key audiences daily— from registration to the classroom to career services—embrace it.

To build internal momentum, foster pride, and boost engagement with [Client Name], Stamats will return to campus for Living the Brand Workshops. These sessions will present engagement findings, discuss tools and strategies, and offer guidance to ensure internal buy-in. We’ll work with leadership and key groups throughout the day, providing training for communications, admissions, advancement teams, and faculty/staff who shape the [Client Name] brand daily. The workshops will include:

A review of [Client Name]'s key messages and themes

Use of the new Brand Book

Highlighting ways the campus community can extend the brand individually and institutionally

Identifying brand champions for further engagement on campus

With years of experience and hundreds of brand clients, we know that broad internal buy-in is key to the success of [Client Name]'s brand project. We've seen schools falter when they launch major promotional efforts without first ensuring internal stakeholders feel ownership and have had a formal preview.

Too often, institutional stakeholders aren't fully engaged or equipped to drive marketing efforts. By prioritizing internal engagement and executing a thoughtful, impactful launch, [Client Name] can significantly amplify its marketing program’s reach and effectiveness.

In addition to the brand rollout summit, we recommend the project team consider the following when planning [Client Name]'s internal brand campaign launch:

Campaign ownership is the first step in animating and rallying the passions of internal stakeholders around the new brand.

Internal engagement enriches the creative process.

A strong internal launch enlightens, informs, and inspires internal stakeholders and converts them into passionate brand ambassadors.

An inspirational internal launch fuels the collective pride and a shared sense of purpose.

To maximize these interactions, we recommend a member of your marketing and communications team participate in all workshops. This will help introduce the Stamats strategist and gather stories that reinforce the brand effort, ensuring that all [Client Name] departments contribute to "living the brand" and strengthening the institution's reputation.

question: Factors that Impact Research Project Timelines
answer:
Factors that impact timelines:

Surveys or qualitative research near major holidays, elections, testing periods, may slow the activity of target audience members and lead to longer data collection periods.

Naming the survey sponsor ([Client Name]) in primary research will increase trust among invited recipients and improve the participation rate.

Timeliness of faculty/staff responses on questions that arise or securing records from the university.

Identifying data sources outside the typical sources used for academic demand assessments may take more time or be inaccessible without a membership or custom fee for syndicated reports, or for custom analysis from accreditation bodies. For highly exploratory projects, this type of initial desk research can extend the schedule. Our experience with requests to accreditation bodies is that receiving results from their proprietary database is a very slow process, even when the request comes from the institution.

Identifying subject matter experts to contact for interviews or a survey if [Client Name] does not have a target list available or a purchased list is inadequate. This is a time intensive task and would incur additional fees.

question: Brand Development Research and Perception Study
answer:

Comprehensive Report & Presentation – A detailed report summarizing research findings, implications for [Client Name]’s brand, and key messaging recommendations, followed by on-campus presentations to internal stakeholders.

question: Client PM Expectations

question: Price Justification for Tuition Pricing Elasticity and Brand Value Study
answer:
Our price for a custom tuition pricing study is reflective of several key factors:

It’s a custom model built for each client based on respondent answers for every study and it’s very labor intensive. The workload cannot be reduced to create a custom model--often more simulations and more adaptations are required for a good statistical fit. This type of work can only be conducted by highly trained analysts and that has a higher price point.

The amount of work behind the scenes to achieve a good participation rate with a blind study has increased considerably. We must often use multiple email distribution systems to send the unique survey URLs to make certain emails are being received. The amount of work has actually increased over the past several years to make sure the emails are hitting their target and we spend more time on data collection than in the past. Audiences of all types are harder to reach and this additional time to manage a blind survey simply costs more today.

answer:

What are the most common questions being asked?

question: Portal Criteria and Process for Removing Offending Members of the Online Community
answer:
The important part of removing members is having a clear guideline in place before the offense occurs. In our strategy prior to launch, Stamats will craft a brief and longform version of your community rules and standards that website visitors must agree to prior to using the portal function.

The guidelines will clearly state situations, conversations, and even specific terms, if appropriate, that will be flagged in the system and may result in banning from the tool or escalated action, if warranted. In these guidelines, considerations such as these will be detailed as ban-worthy offenses from the site/tool:

Threatening language directed at the hospital, patients, providers, faculty, or staff

Racist, sexist, ableist, or religious epithets and other hate speech as defined by your organization

Sexual solicitation language

Substance abuse language outside requests for care

At your discretion, we can implement a warning system or enact swift removal protocols. Stamats will review feedback reports regularly and use administrative functions to remove or ban harmful individuals and bots from the site.

question: Content Creation: Content and SEO
answer:
Content in Practice

Great design starts the conversion journey—strong content finishes it. Your redesigned site will need strategic, updated content that aligns with user goals and supports the new design.

Whether your team owns content creation or needs extra support, Stamats can step in as needed—offering flexible content services during both the build and ongoing maintenance phases.

Content Planning: Writing, Optimizing & Migrating at Scale

We group website content into three categories: new, optimized, or migrated as-is. Based on past projects, a typical breakdown is 30% new, 40% optimized, and 30% migrated or archived.

Large sites often mean large content efforts—but we have the tools and expertise to help you manage it efficiently.

Write Faster: Use Stamats’ UX Writers

In past projects, Stamats’ writing team has delivered content in about half the time of internal teams. For example, while two full-time client writers might take six weeks to draft 200 pages, Stamats typically completes the same in under a month.

Our UX Content Writing Approach

Every page we create is goal-driven and aligned with the user journey. We write with web behavior in mind—scannable, accessible, and action-oriented content that supports your brand and meets usability standards.

All content is:

Accurate and on-brand

Clear, concise, and web-optimized

Aligned with tone, reading level, and accessibility standards

Tailored to your audiences, from high school prospects to adult learners

Our UX writers follow your content strategy and use design elements to enhance engagement. We deliver content at scale—efficiently and flexibly—adapting to your team’s review process.

Write Earlier, Sprint Often: Accelerated Content Pathway

Stamats knows content can be the most time-consuming part of a project. Our Accelerated Content Pathway streamlines the process—allowing us to begin writing before final design, using your new strategy and minimizing rewrites.

We work in agile sprints, typically two weeks per content block. This keeps workloads manageable and feedback cycles short—boosting both speed and quality.

New & Optimized Content

New content may require SME interviews, surveys, or research. Whether handled by your team or ours, we use guided questions to uncover unique perspectives and stories that reflect[Client Name]and resonate with your audiences. These aren’t rigid scripts—they're tools to gather the most relevant, user-focused information. For related content sets, we ensure consistency while maintaining clarity for users.

Support for Your Content Team

We equip your team to write, edit, and curate content, from launch of the new design through ongoing maintenance cycles. We offer practical support through:

Content Template (AKA page table): A structured guide (usually delivered as a Word doc) to the content needed in a use-case (e.g., academic program page, faculty profile, or office home page).

UX Content Style Guide: An all-in-one guide to writing and editing content, designed for a distributed network of web contributors. The Style Guide includes:

Content strategy assets

Editorial style variations for the web

Recommendations for creating and maintaining effective web content

Writing for the Web Training: Customized to your team’s strengths and delivered in short workshops (up to 5). One of these workshops can be customized for content owners and reviewers, to help them understand the ways web content differs from print and academic content.

SEO Strategy and Keyword Research

In today’s digital environment, nothing stands alone. Design, content, UX, SEO, and branding must work together seamlessly. At Stamats, we create integrated solutions that ensure your digital presence is effective, discoverable, and user-friendly across platforms and devices.

We go beyond basic keyword research to understand:

How your audiences talk about [Client Name]

What questions they ask that you can answer

Common terms used for your programs

Our holistic design and development process ensures your site isn’t just beautiful—it’s optimized for visibility and usability. We consider key SEO and content questions, such as:

How keywords and content structure impact ranking

How site architecture supports indexing and future content

How links and updates drive lasting search performance

question: Information Architecture and Content Strategy: Content Strategy and Practice
answer:
Delivering Content Strategy

Your new site has a job: contribute to your institutional goals. Your new site must align with your strategic plan, institutional priorities, your brand, your business goals, and your community values. In addition, the plans for your new site must work well with your technical infrastructure and your team’s skillset. Based on discussions and workshops throughout Discovery, we articulate a guiding strategy for the site’s content that informs design, content, and migration.

We develop and deliver this strategy via tools that make sense for your project. Strategy development begins on day one. High-level, site-wide strategy is usually delivered at the Discovery Audit presentation:

Strategy Setting Statement: A phrase or sentence that defines the work of the site’s content (Discovery Audit). The strategy statement becomes the standard for the more detailed findings of our discovery activities:

Audience Maps: Documenting your priority audiences (Discovery Audit)

Journey Map: Documenting your top audience’s three-phase journey of consideration, clarification, and conversion (Discovery Audit)

Voice and Tone Map: A shared guide to the pace, personality, and emotional tone of the content (copy and images), drawing on existing brand resources

Personas: A set of character stories, built on your audience segments, to bring depth and empathy to content choices. Each persona includes demographic, biographic, and psychographic information

High Level Content Audit: A review of the content breadth, depth, and quality on your existing site. Includes a content inventory, delivered as a first-draft content matrix

As we move from discovery to design, the strategy takes shape in these deliverables:

Information Architecture/High-Level Site Map: The overall pattern for organizing and accessing content within your site

Navigation Strategy: The high-level elements of persistent and universal menus, icons, and navigational areas (e.g., footers, search bars, etc.), as well as our navigational UX goal (e.g., “minimalist”)

Content Priority Guides: One for each design template

Model Content: A limited set of pages, revised and adapted for this site’s strategy and design. May have placeholder content if additional research is needed for profiles or facts. Minimally, we develop model content for each template/use-case layout.

As we move closer to development, content strategy becomes more granular. We deliver this more focused strategy in the form that makes sense for the complexity, functionality, and structure of your site and its content:

Optimization Strategy: A chart or map of dynamic content elements and how they will be deployed on the site. The strategy will also define:

Taxonomy Approach: Which structures in the CMS (e.g., tags, categories, etc.) will be used to organize and filter content libraries.

Personalization Plan: Where will personalized content appear in the user journey, what is the logic of the personalization, and what content do we need.

Shared Content Libraries: Identifying the starter collections of content that will feed dynamically into different pages (e.g., news, profiles, events, testimonials, images, factoids, calls-to-action, deadline callouts, etc.). May also identify starter blocks of content, which begin with shared content but are edited on the page and do not update dynamically.

question: Clive Experience
answer:
Stamats is a premier Cascade partner, and our established partnership means our joint clients get the full benefits of the CMS. We understand how reusable content and shared content work within Cascade and how to leverage new features such as Clive. If your vendor of choice does not set up the foundation specifically for Cascade and Clive, it will require rework. Stamats was one of the first partners to begin implementing Clive features.

Effective personalization begins with a clear understanding of the user journey and ensuring every touchpoint—both current and repeat visits—is connected.

At its core, personalization uses a series of “if/then” paths to guide users while still offering the flexibility to explore other content. It’s about prioritizing—not hiding—information, creating a more intuitive experience.

Launching personalization means committing to ongoing testing and refinement. While it offers valuable insights, regular review ensures users stay engaged and on track. This continuous investment builds a seamless, personalized experience over time.

question: Confidential Personally Identifiable Data Handling Policy
answer:
Stamats' data handling policy for personally identifiable data is built around the following guidelines:

Perform security measures to protect personal data from being accessed by non-authorized parties

Use data exclusively for intended purpose

Destroy all client records that contain sensitive data after delivery

Use encryption technologies when transferring data between parties

Require vendors to follow confidential data handling policy

File Transfer Procedure

Stamats’ file transfer protocol is designed around the following safeguards:

Use secure client transfer system where applicable

Require 256 bit encryption when sending electronically

Expire/destroy data availability once transaction is complete

If [Client Name] does not have a preferred data transfer system in place, Stamats can provide a file transfer service.

question: Regular Internal Security Tests and/or Audits
answer:
The Involta SOC provides clients with a dedicated security team that monitors and analyzes their security posture constantly, ensuring a proactive approach to security threats and intrusions.

Vulnerability scans

Alert Logic performs internal network-based vulnerability scans weekly. This finds vulnerabilities in hosts that do not support an agent, such as a firewall or layer three device.

Alert Logic’s agents are scanning hosts daily for application and security vulnerabilities, such as missing Windows OS patches, Apache vulnerabilities, etc.

Alert Logic is scanning the perimeter network (external) weekly.

SentinelOne is also performing vulnerability scans on the host to report on application vulnerabilities.

Application security assessments

Alert Logic uses web application exploit modules in their vulnerability scans. This includes testing for SQL injection attacks, input validation exploits, XSS exploits, etc.

Penetration tests

We currently do not offer white, black, or gray box penetration testing. We recommend going with a third-party on this, which could be Trace3 or IANS.

Penetration tests are usually more “hands on keyboard” and will provide more in-depth information than vulnerability scanning can. This typically involves someone running specific exploit modules, performing social engineering attacks on your users, and attempting to steal credentials while moving laterally throughout the network. These are usually scheduled annually for most companies and include comprehensive reporting.

question: Describe your experience in building an intranet platform
answer:
Focusing on audience needs, our UX and digital experience support internet, intranet, and portal websites.

Our team has developed intranets in WordPress, Cascade, Modern Campus CMS, SharePoint, Drupal, and Sitecore.

Understanding the target audience, as with any site, is the key to an intranet foundation. What tasks do they need to accomplish and how can the site assist in an easy-to-complete experience. One of the tests we use is “Could a new employee or current student find this?” If looking for a travel reimbursement form (or a change of major form) requires knowing which department created it, then the answer is no.

When crafting intranet strategies, our workshops and recommendations focus on:

Repeatable IA structures: Consistency from department to department with flexibility to support their unique content.

Removing internal knowledge: Employees and students should be able to understand where key/shared information lives based on intuitive navigation, not legacy organizational knowledge.

“Single Source” index: Where should each piece of content live? How do we eliminate duplication? What introductory copy do we need to point them to that source? Should the source be searchable in the overall index?

Governance: One of the biggest risks of an intranet investment is the lack of adherence to the structure and a new site that quickly degrades. Our workshops focus on crafting intuitive structures that support a natural adherence as well as operational processes.

Single Sign-in and System Integration: Intranets are key information hubs but they do not replace all internal applications. Our technical and UX team partner to make sure a shared experience is crafted as users interact with the site.
