question: Intranet
answer:
Introduction

Focusing on audience needs, our UX and digital experience support internet, intranet, and portal websites.

Our team has developed intranets in Cascade, Modern Campus CMS, SharePoint, Sitecore, WordPress, and Drupal.

Understanding the audience needs, as with any site, is the key to an intranet foundation. What tasks do they need to accomplish and how can the site assist in an easy-to-complete experience. One of the tests we use is “Could a new employee or current student find this?” If looking for a travel reimbursement form (or a change of major form) requires knowing which department created it, then the answer is no.

When crafting intranet strategies, our workshops and recommendations focus on:

Repeatable IA structures: Consistency from department to department with flexibility to support their unique content.

Removing internal knowledge: Employees and students should be able to understand where key/shared information lives based on intuitive navigation, not legacy organizational knowledge.

“Single Source” index: Where should each piece of content live? How do we eliminate duplication? What introductory copy do we need to point them to that source? Should the source be searchable in the overall index?

Governance: One of the biggest risks of an intranet investment is the lack of adherence to the structure and a new site that quickly degrades. Our workshops focus on crafting intuitive structures that support a natural adherence as well as operational processes.

Single Sign-in and System Integration: Intranets are key information hubs, but they do not replace all internal applications. Our technical and UX team partner to make sure a shared experience is crafted as users interact with the site.

[Client Name]’s Intranet Project Approach

Our proposal focuses on Discovery, research, information architecture, user experience design, and front-end development. Strategy is where Stamats shines.

The Project

Using Modern Campus CMS or Cascade, Stamats will create a site architecture and design, keeping in mind the functionality of the CMS and tying design requirements to native functionality. With strong visuals and a highly organized information architecture, the site will support the goal of managing the intended internal audience and creating a wall between internal needs and external audiences.

Strategy, Discovery, and Site Purpose

Intranet strategy begins with understanding the purpose of the site and how it connects to many other internal applications. During our Discovery, Stamats will lead an evaluation of each type of content and where that content should live. We call this our True Source Mapping. Once we know which applications are needed (within or outside the intranet), we can begin to lay out the structure.

During this phase, the team also meets with the IT administrator to discuss technical requirements, reusable elements, and user profile experiences.

We will deliver this via the following meetings or workshops:

Kickoff and planning

Walk through of current site content and functionality

Walk through of IT environment (with IT team)

True Source Mapping

Identification of single sign-on integrations

Strategy Setting Statement: A phrase or sentence that defines the work of the intranet’s content.

Content Strategy

The new intranet has one key job: to support the college’s internal communication efforts. In our work with intranets, we find typical goals include providing employees and students with information quickly and efficiently; increasing engagement among employees to cultivate a sense of community and purpose; enabling usage tracking and data; and encouraging departments to easily contribute and distribute successful content. In addition to these goals and those we discuss with the [Client Name] team, the plans for your new site must work well with your technical infrastructure and your team’s skillset. Based on Discovery efforts, we articulate a guiding strategy for the site’s content that informs design, content, and migration.

One of the most important things about intranet content is defining the purpose of the content. The second is making sure the content lives in the right site. There is often confusion about where content should live and if this isn’t defined, the site will struggle from confusion and unnecessary bloat.

Information Architecture, Navigation, and Wireframe

Stamats IA experts will help your team sort through your intranet content to determine the most useful categories of content for your internal teams. For example, the goal of this section is to make the site usable for a new employee or new student, even when they don’t understand the college’s organizational chart. The intranet can and should be a tool for those new to the institution to find the supporting information they need.

Upon confirming those categories—based on topics and audience needs—we will recommend a sitemap structure up to three levels deep to house your content. For department specific content, Stamats will recommend a standard sitemap for consistency across departments.

As part of this scope of work, we will provide a strategy and definition of how to evaluate content, as well as a clear direction on what content lives within departments versus shared.

We deliver this strategy via:

Audience Task Identification: The question or need that brings them to the intranet, as well as the decision(s) they make to continue.

Information Architecture/High-Level Site Map: The overall pattern for organizing and accessing content within the intranet. Assumes two to three sorting workshops. Evaluation of how[Client Name]/portal/ integrates into the site.

Policies: Workshop to outline categories and sorting/filter options for the sitemap structure. Evaluation of a sample of policies is recommended to understand the different types.

Department Specific Site Map: A repeatable sitemap that addresses most departments.

Navigation Strategy: The high-level elements of persistent and universal menus, icons, and navigational areas (e.g., footers, search bars, etc.), as well as our navigational UX goal (e.g., “minimalist”).

Homepage Priorities: An outline or map of the key content elements for the site’s homepage.

Department Layout: A wireframe outline for departments.

Section Layout: A wireframe for use for key section pages.

Up to five department interviews (up to an hour each) to discuss content needs and present Stamats recommend approach.

Optional: Content writing for department pages after the interview with one round of revision.

Executive or All Stakeholder meeting: Presentation of the sitemap approach and wireframe for internal buy-in.

Intranet Design

Beginning with the intranet homepage, our design workshop brings the wireframe and navigation strategy to life.

Following your style guide and our design preference workshop, Stamats will create an intranet home, department, section, and two sample audience designs for the site with two rounds of reviews. The intranet design can include functions and features such as:

Logo or name

Main navigation

Footer

Sub-navigation for interior pages

Feature call outs

Tabs and accordions

Tables

Photo sliders

Large pull quote area

Single photo feature

Stat/factoid box(es)

Body copy blocks

Policy search

The templates will be designed for use within Modern Campus CMS or Cascade (whichever is selected). Documentation on image sizing and usage will be provided.

We will deliver this strategy via:

Home page design

Department page design

Section page design

Two sample design pages

Design style guide

Development guide

Assumptions

Stamats deliverables include sitemap and design.

For specific site content work, an additional proposal is required.

[Client Name]will select images for the site.

Site will be designed for use in the chosen CMS.

Assumes [Client Name]will manage development, migration, and security roles.

Stamats will be granted appropriate access to the Butte intranet to plan, evaluate, and strategize as part of this scope of work.

question: Cloud Hosting Benefits
answer:
Cloud hosting offers scalable, reliable performance through load-balanced, clustered servers. It's more resilient than traditional hosting—if one server fails, others take over seamlessly. Decentralization also reduces the impact of local outages or disasters.

Unlike flat-rate or hardware-heavy models, cloud hosting uses utility-based billing, so you only pay for the resources you actually use—making it both cost-effective and efficient.

question: Experience Map and Onsite Interviews
answer:
An experience map is a visual representation of your patient’s actual experience before, during, and after a visit to your clinic. A physical map projects what patients and visitors feel and think every step of the way. Though the end product is a digital in-clinic experience, the map takes the full journey, bookending touchpoints into account so the resulting work will have depth and context. This activity is also an excellent team alignment tool, creating a common baseline for validating or rejecting design decisions.

The Stamats team will work through a detailed and illuminating experience strategy phase:

Half-day experience map workshop to define touchpoints, motivators, and emotions for multiple personas

Up to five interviews (suggested meetings with: Bariatric, IBD, GERD, Integrative, etc.)

Tour of the facility

In addition, Stamats recommends a patient success interview, which can be conducted remotely.

We deliver this strategy via:

On site workshop and preparation

Industry and competitive research

High fidelity experience map

It would be our intention to schedule the onsite as soon as possible.

Cost: $29,000

question: Accessible PDFs and PDF Strategy
answer:
Content Strategy and PDFs

PDFs may seem like an easy content solution, but they present several challenges:

Difficult to read on mobile devices, limiting accessibility for a large portion of users.

Often fail to meet accessibility standards, hindering users with vision, cognitive, or hearing impairments.

May negatively impact search engine visibility and site search accuracy.

We offer PDF accessibility training using Acrobat Pro for testing and conversion. Alternatively, Stamats can convert PDFs at an additional cost.

PDF Accessibility Training Agenda

The goal is to help your team decide when and how to make PDFs accessible, not just how to convert them.

Training covers:

Decision tree: Purpose, use, and necessity of PDFs.

ADA compliance: Logical reading order, title requirements, color contrast, tagged content, and accessible links.

Testing PDFs for ADA compliance, including audio descriptions.

Hands-on Workshop:

Training on making PDFs accessible with Acrobat Pro.

Handling image-only PDFs, multimedia, and forms.

Training Structure:

Two 90-minute sessions.

Session 1: Overview of PDFs and ADA compliance.

Session 2: Hands-on testing and conversion with Acrobat Pro (requires installation and sample PDFs).

Pricing Structure

PDF Options

Training Package: Workshop and Documentation

$3,200

PDF Conversion

Stamats will convert 10 PDFs to accessible PDFs. If the PDFs have custom images or tables, those source images would need to be converted by the Client.

In set of 10 PDFs

$2,700

question: Process(es) to ensure websites meet WCAG Guidelines 2.2 Level AA
answer:
We design with accessibility in mind, following these key practices:

Ensure all templates include a clear H1 and a logical heading structure.

Verify color combinations meet contrast requirements before use.

Indicate if image overlays are needed in CSS for text accessibility.

Consider visual impairments, including color blindness.

Conduct initial tests during the first round of template development, followed by revalidation post-development.

Testing Tools and Methods:

Automated Reports: WAVE (Chrome/Firefox), W3C Markup Validation.

Manual Tests: Color contrast (WebAIM), keyboard navigation, screen reader accessibility.

Content Guidelines:

Best practices for alt text, heading structure, and link text.

Ongoing user training throughout the project.

Accessibility considerations included in all content guidelines.

Ongoing Audit:We offer an optional service for continuous accessibility monitoring.

question: Design compatibility with assistive technologies tested for blind/visually impaired
answer:
Our web team ensures that the design is compatible with assistive technologies through:

Color Contrast – using the WebAIM contrast checking for colors used within the design.

Screen Reader – manually access content using common screen reader technology.

Color Blindness combinations considered during the design phase.

question: Accessibility Validation
answer:
Stamats adheres to WCAG 2.2 AA, Section 508, and ADA standards to ensure accessibility without compromising design or functionality.

Throughout production, we use automated tools (e.g., WAVE, color contrast checkers, screen readers) and manual reviews to validate HTML accessibility. Identified issues are resolved before delivery, and your team receives full documentation of testing results.

All designs and templates undergo Stamats’ rigorous seven-step QA process, which includes:

Documentation review

Application walkthrough and sanity check

Test case creation

Issue reporting

End-to-end testing

UI feedback and detailed results

Final QA report to project lead

Finally, we also offer Stamats HTML Template Quality Testing Statement.

The current browsers and devices included in testing are:

Windows: Current versions of Firefox, Chrome, Internet Explorer, and Edge. Legacy browser support (older than five years) is not covered unless otherwise requested

iOS (last two stable versions): Default browser

Android (last two stable versions): Default browser

Adherence to design, requirements, and specifications documentation

General usability

Functionality

Best Practices for the web

Validation:

W3C

question: Coding Standards and Validation
answer:
Our team develops using the latest standards—HTML5, CSS3, PHP, and .NET—to ensure modern functionality, security, and consistent styling across platforms. Templates follow logical, maintainable style sheets and pass W3C HTML validation.

Functional templates will be presented to [Client Name]for review and approval, along with a detailed functionality and style guide.

We follow WCAG 2.2 AA guidelines and meet Section 508 and ADA standards, balancing accessibility with modern design. During production, we use automated tools, color contrast checkers, and screen readers, then validate and correct any issues. Final testing documentation will be provided to your team.

Our QA team applies a rigorous, proprietary multi-step process to ensure consistent quality, including:

Documentation review and sanity checks

QA documentation and test case creation

End-to-end testing and UI recommendations

Final QA report to project lead

At project handoff, the website will meet WCAG 2.2 A and AA standards. Ongoing accessibility compliance will be the responsibility of [Client Name].

answer:
Does your product undergo accessibility testing prior to each major release?

Yes, we do an accessibility review at the following phases:

Content produced by Stamats is written to follow the WCAG guidelines for content.

question: HTML Template Build: Accessibility Testing
answer:
Stamats designs with accessibility at the forefront, meeting Section 508 and ADA requirements by following WCAG 2.2 AA standards—balancing modern design with full functionality.

During production, we use automated tools (WAVE, color contrast checkers, screen readers) and manual testing (keyboard navigation, tab order, screen reader verification) to ensure compliance. We validate HTML and correct any accessibility issues before final delivery. Your team will receive documentation of all testing and results.

Accessibility testing includes:

Homepage and full-component page evaluations

Logical heading structures and accessible H1 usage

Brand color contrast verification and image overlay recommendations

ADA-compliant video captioning: max three lines, 99% accuracy, Helvetica-style font, sound cues in brackets

Ongoing testing during development and post-launch

Content and training support includes:

Best practices for headings, alt text, and link labels

Accessibility-integrated content guidelines and user training

Optional ongoing audits for continuous compliance monitoring

Addressing Issues

Accessibility issues are tracked in our bug system with an “Accessibility” tag. Any compliance issue found on a live production page is marked “High Priority.”

We test for WCAG 2.2 AA compliance, addressing all issues within A and AA levels. Fixes are prioritized based on impact to the user experience and your business goals. High-priority items are resolved first to ensure a smooth launch, with lower-tier issues addressed in order—without delaying rollout.

question: Analysis, Goal Setting, and Education
answer:
The [Client Name] website is an important communications platform for both students and the community, plus a significant contributor to new student acquisition. Stamats helps the [Client Name] team assess the site’s ability to perform its communication and acquisition tasks through site analytics, content assessment and optimization, and user experience strategies.

Google Analytics 4 (GA4) is the primary platform used to measure visitor activity and behaviors on the website. Beyond daily or weekly routine maintenance, Stamats begins each quarter with a “best practices” audit of all tools related to GA4. The goal is to ensure accurate and complete data collection. The audit includes GA4, Tag Manager, Search Console (for organic search), and five different Looker Studio dashboards.

KPIs and conversion goals help tell the full story of visitor engagement with the site. Stamats conducted our “KPI and Goals” workshop with the Client Name team during the initial setup of GA4. Since then, quarterly reviews with recommendations have kept KPI tracking up to date. Whenever the Client Name team has requested a change or update, Stamats has completed the request—frequently in as little as one hour.

Customized Looker Studio dashboards provide near-real-time access to analytics data. Stamats created five core dashboards with many customized one-of-a-kind charts and graphs. Beyond 24/7 data availability, the dashboards provide information faster and without the need for specialized skills compared to navigating through GA4’s reports, filters, and segments.

The most valuable thing about data is its analysis. Stamats reporting makes data understandable and actionable by relying on a simple and proven three-part structure:

How did the site earn its visitors and from where?

What behaviors did site visitors exhibit?

What did Client Name get from the visitor’s time on the site?

Acquisition, Behavior, Outcome. These three questions can be used to analyze any segment of visitors, e.g., Organic Search Visitors, Mobile Device Visitors, and Highly Engaged Visitors. The Stamats team then layers in our understanding of the higher education market, its drivers and pitfalls, and how prospective student behaviors vary by region across the country.

Our goal with every report is to provide actionable insights that can enhance site performance, the visitor’s experience, and outcomes for the institution.

Along with Analytics reporting, using service hours Stamats can create usability reports, content analysis, and user journeys for one or more segments of the site. Our research includes many tools from visit heatmapping to content language analysis.

After a deep analysis, Stamats offers solutions for content and design that uniquely match [Client Name]’s needs. And of course, we benchmark performance before any changes and analyze outcomes after the updates are live and on the site.

Stamats offers a wide range of workshops that appeal to marketers, web managers, and stakeholders. Education not only fosters shared knowledge among interconnected teams, but it is also the best way to achieve buy-in when change is possible or expected.

question: How Proposed Redesign Services Will Achieve Result Requirements: Integrate Content to Provide a Targeted Experience
answer:
Integrate content to provide individual user types with targeted marketing and functional information and in addition, complement offline marketing collateral.

Taxonomy as a Core Content Strategy

A well-structured taxonomy is essential to your content strategy. It supports dynamic page creation by automatically surfacing relevant, up-to-date content across your site. We'll identify core content types—such as news, events, faculty bios, testimonials, and contact info—and define tags (e.g., academic programs, audiences, or topics) to ensure content reaches the right users in the right context.

Benefits of Taxonomy:

Delivers relevant content dynamically, rather than keeping it siloed

Centralizes content governance (e.g., news auto-populates in news sections)

Reduces duplication and outdated content

Our approach connects related content through shared taxonomy and structured content types. For example, academic programs can be linked to news, events, testimonials, faculty, and location data—ensuring consistent, current information throughout the site.

We also support cloning, which enables content (like a directions page) to appear in multiple sections without disrupting navigation or SEO—ensuring contextual relevance and usability.

Finally, taxonomy informs the modular design of your site, shaping both visual hierarchy and how content is assembled across pages.

question: Department Toolkits - for Templates, Content, Unit Training, etc.
answer:
Department Toolkits

In order to support a large and/or decentralized institution, a key strategy is departmental roll-out and support. Running a web project and only focusing on the main structure or treating the project as a single launch creates confusion, lack of buy-in, and departmental frustration. All of this leads to lack of adherence to the new templates and excessive and unnecessary 1:1 communication for the marketing team.

Our approach provides support for the departments and the marketing team. Our toolkit is specifically designed for large, complex, distributed sites that want to provide flexibility yet centralized control.

The toolkit includes:

Sample sitemaps for up to five areas, such as a college, department, research center, or major office

Sample recommendations for menu labels and content topics for each of the five different sitemap examples

Provide training kit to address content matrix and how to apply it to their site

Template toolkit detailing sample pages, example content storytelling, detailed image instructions and documented content templates

SEO training and documentation

Accessibility training for content, images and PDFs and documentation

Guidelines on URL structures, CMS component naming conventions, and image location documentation

Four online training sessions for departments to attend

Up to five short videos on how to use the templates for content development

One Department Leadership presentation

question: Online Bulletin Board Discussion Group
answer:
To explore [insert scope of exploration], we recommend an online bulletin board discussion, which is a qualitative research method that facilitates a guided discussion with [target audience] and the moderator over a three-day period. Some refer to this method of market research as a message board or online community. The online bulletin board is a moderated, asynchronous discussion with 12-15 participants recruited, and pre-screened and pre-recruited participants who interact with the moderator and other participants through the mobile platform in guided discussion with activities, questions, and polls.

This approach allows participants to upload videos, images, and files to give more context to the topic or mark-up images provided for the discussion. This approach is suitable for geographically dispersed or hard-to-reach audiences and allows participants to respond when its suits their schedule on a daily basis over the 3-day engagement. Observers from [Client Name]may chat with the moderator in a back-room chat area that is completely separate from the area accessed by participants. Client observers may follow all of the action in real-time over the three-day period if they desire.

Through the online bulletin board discussion, Stamats could cover the following topics:

Understand [target audience’s] motivation to seek this service

Identify emotions [target audience] express in the journey or process and establish key challenges

Brainstorm, storyboard, enable ideation exercises to find solutions your [client] team has not considered

Capture immediate reactions to your ads, website, concept description, copy, images, etc…

Below is a screen image of what a bulletin board platform looks like to a participant:

INSERT BULLETIN BOARD IMAGE

In a kick-off meeting, Stamats and [Client Name] will establish the project’s goals, desired topics for discussion, and outcomes. During this meeting a schedule will be set for recruitment, data collection and reporting. It’s important for Stamats to receive any visuals, videos or other stimuli that may be used in the explorative engagement. Reviewing this with you, we will have a better understanding of how this will inform your project outcomes and next steps.

After this meeting, Stamats creates a screener survey to select participants who would best be suited for the online bulletin board discussion. Once you approve the written screener survey, Stamats may begin recruitment for the board. For this engagement, we assume Stamats will be able to conduct the initial screening from the list provided by the client using email invitations to a web survey. If the [name of target audience, student, facility manager] qualifies and agrees to participate for the three-day board, the platform link is sent with instructions. Incentives are stated in the screener and distributed at the board's closing.

Incentives are essential for this audience and have been included in our fees. Incentives are necessary to improve commitment to the three-day board and thank respondents for their time. Stamats handles all incentive distribution. For this particular project, we suggest a [$xxxx] incentive fee to cover all participants.

Stamats services include:

Recruitment screener development and recruitment

Creation of moderation guide

Programming and hosting of the online platform in a secure environment

Live moderation of the bulletin board

Client observation on the platform

Reminder emails 48-72 hours prior to the board's start

Tech-checks with all recruited participants one day prior to the start of the board

Automatic transcription and capture of all activities in the discussion

Distribution of incentives

Analysis and reporting of the bulletin board with summary report including verbatim comments, polls of key moments

60-minute online presentation of summary report with your core team

Client responsibilities include:

Contact list for recruitment

Approval of screener and moderation guide

Online bulletin boards can provide rich insights in a relatively short time period with your key target audience. A typical timeline may look like this:

Week 1:  Initial call, discuss screener, gain approval, set project objectives so recruitment can begin

Week 2-3: Recruitment, moderation guide approved and programmed.

Week 4: Online discussion

Week 5-6: Analysis and delivery of report

question: In-Depth Interviews
answer:
To uncover insights on [attitudes, motivations for purchase decision, perceptions towards the brand and identify barriers to brand selection], we suggest in-depth interviews, also referred to as IDIs with [INSERT AUDIENCES]. This qualitative component is conducted by telephone or online conference call (Zoom) with persons who [specify characteristics assumed for screener criteria]. Stamats would recruit through [insert source of sample] to achieve up to [xx] qualitative interviews lasting up to [xx] minutes.

In-depth interviews are frequently used to reveal the thoughts behind specific behaviors when exploring a new topic, provide direction on tone and word choice by the target audience, and explore issues in greater detail. [Subject matter experts, influencers, or key business leaders often have volumes of information to share on a given topic and individual interviews are often the best way to fit their schedule.] Interviews are semi-structured discussions between the moderator and participant and allow for flexible and interactive question techniques. While a discussion or moderation guide is used to frame the conversation, the moderator can probe where needed to unpack highly relevant and useful insights.

[The insights from the interviews will be used to develop attributes and questions to guide the next phase of the research- the larger quantitative web survey.]

question: Stamats Privacy Policy
answer:
Stamats Standard Privacy Policy

Today, where privacy has become of intense concern, we take your privacy very seriously. The privacy and security of your personal data (“Personal Information”) which we collect from you is important to us. It is equally important that you understand how we handle this data. Stamats will not knowingly collect or use Personal Information in any manner not consistent with this policy, as it may be amended from time to time, and applicable laws.

Collection of Information

In the course of conducting our business and complying with federal, state, and local government regulations governing such matters as employment, tax, insurance, etc., we must collect Personal Information from you. The nature of the information collected varies somewhat for each employee, depending on your employment responsibilities, the location of the facility where you work, and other factors. We collect Personal Information from you solely for business purposes, including those related directly to your employment with Stamats, and those required by governmental agencies.

Use of the Information Collected

The primary purposes for collection, storage, and/or use of your Personal Information include, but are not limited to:

Human Resources Management. We collect, store, analyze, and share (internally) Personal Information to attract, retain, and motivate a highly qualified workforce. This includes recruiting, compensation planning, succession planning, reorganization needs, performance assessment, training, employee benefit administration, compliance with applicable legal requirements, and communication with employees and/or their representatives.

Business Processes and Management. Personal Information is used to run our business operations including, for example, scheduling work assignments, managing Stamats assets, reporting and/or releasing public data (e.g., annual reports, etc.), and populating employee directories. Information may also be used to comply with government regulation.

Safety and Security Management. We use such Personal Information as appropriate to ensure the safety and protection of employees, assets, resources, and communities.

Communication and Identification. We use your Personal Information to identify you and to communicate with you.

Limited Disclosure: Stamats acts to protect your Personal Information and ensure that unauthorized individuals do not have access to such information by using security measures to protect Personal Information.

We will not knowingly disclose, sell, or otherwise distribute your Personal Information to any third party without your knowledge and, where appropriate, your express written permission, except where disclosure is reasonably necessary to comply with the law.

Security of Personal Information

We employ reasonable security measures and technologies, such as password protection, encryption, physical locks, etc., to protect the confidentiality of your Personal Information. Only authorized employees have access to Personal Information. Employees with such authorization must take the appropriate safeguards to protect such information. Paper and other hard copy containing Personal Information (or any other confidential information) are secured in a locked location when not in use. Computers and other access points are secured when not in use by logging out or locking. Passwords and user IDs are guarded and not shared. When no longer necessary for business purposes, paper and hard copies are immediately destroyed using paper shredders or similar devices. We do not leave copies in unsecured locations waiting to be shredded or otherwise destroyed. We do not make or distribute unauthorized copies of documents or other tangible mediums containing Personal Information. Electronic files containing Personal Information are stored only on secure computers and not copied or otherwise shared with unauthorized individuals within or outside of Stamats.

Stamats will make reasonable efforts to secure Personal Information stored or transmitted electronically from hackers or other persons who are not authorized to access such information. Any violation or potential violation of this policy should be reported to your immediate supervisor, designated manager, or Human Resources. The failure by any employee to follow these privacy policies may result in discipline up to and including discharge of the employee. Any questions or suggestions regarding this policy may also be directed to your immediate supervisor, designated manager, or Human Resources.

question: Website Inspiration
answer:
Inspiration for this Project

We find inspiration everywhere! While we work predominately in higher ed, we also engage with healthcare, B2B, and consumer organizations. Our team collects and shares emerging trends, superb functionality, target audience-centricity, and journey-focused approaches.

We’re happy to share a few sites that have surfaced recently, as well as some perennial favorites. We sort these by emerging or intriguing experiences.

Simplified Navigation

Dakota State: A higher ed site that many find appealing for its fixed, simplified side navigation. Content takeovers keep users on the page.

Design Museum: Lots of drama in a simple space.

Style Novels: Simplified, almost to the point of mystery. Content loads after a selection. Could be an interesting inspiration for a program finder.

Scroll Effects

Ocean School: We love how this school uses the scroll.

Le Commandant Charcot: An even more seamless scroll.

Unit London: Art gallery site with an immersive take on the standard hero carousel. Simple nav, simple homepage.

Cards

Not an emerging trend, for sure, but beautiful cards still work wonders.

The Met Exhibitions: The art of the oversized card

Clear, User-Centered Sites

OK, these are ours. Yet people we talk with often cite them out of the blue. (We kind of like that.)

Western University’s Admission Microsite

Kirkwood Community College

University of Florida: The site’s key mission was to support the reputation of UFL and increase the university’s U.S. News and World Report ranking to the top 5—which we accomplished!

University of Illinois: Forward presentation of news stories.

University of New Mexico The Pulse

Thoughts for the [Client Name]Redesign

We know that first impressions are crucial. Beyond local and regional competitors, national online providers offer stiff competition and often large budgets. Your site is competing against institutions with aggressive ad campaigns and effective landing pages. Therefore, [Client Name]must “sell” in the very first impression. We know you will need to:

Have a clear call-to-action design approach.

Be optimized for SEO performance.

Be able to clearly track funnel engagement.

Offer personalization for custom content based on user behaviors, user location, user device, and visit history. By creating targeted messages to audiences, similar to Amazon and retail promotions, [Client Name]can better support a nurture strategy and optimize the real estate of your site.

Through it all, your site must reflect your brand, your values, and the [Client Name]experience (and demonstrate why you're a great choice). Sites can support brand in content tone, design, typeface, and imagery. It should feel like the experience the student will have in their program.

question: Discuss how your firm approaches outreach to historically underserved markets in higher education.
answer:
Our approach is based on a customized partnership with each client. Individual institutional goals and KPIs vary and discussions on campus influence the direction outreach may take. Based on the defined underserved audiences, we provide an array of options to reach students and their families.

For instance, data from digital campaigns can provide insight into the preferred language of an audience reached. Stamats' communication tools such as SMS have resulted in 80% of campaign leads responding to questionnaires in Spanish. This response data helped us provide guidance for our client to expand their campaign reach through Spanish messaging. Providing messaging in an alternative language helps students easily find—and act on—the information they need.

Stamats will strategize with you to define sometimes-overlooked audiences, such as first-generation students, who encompass a large segment of underserved target audiences. Providing parallel marketing and communications in their languages helps students include parents and family members who are decision-influencers about attending college. Strategic collateral recommendations could include:

Email campaigns that drive to a Spanish page on cost of attendance and admission steps

Print pieces for recruitment in English on one side and Spanish or another language as needed on the other

Admit letters in English on one side and another language as needed on the other

Throughout the process of creating marketing messages for search, social, and communications, we seek to guide our schools on choosing images that are inclusive and represent who they are, capturing the authenticity of the school’s demographics to tell the story of your school and who attends; this can also include non-traditional students. The stories told through imagery show prospective students how they fit in while learning about key steps to attending.

A major part of planning effective ads and landing pages is using the terminology your audience uses—not internal, institutional vernacular. Particularly for historically underserved audiences, we must make careful, intentional choices for wording in headings and sentences as well as on conversion points such as inline links, buttons, and menu labels.

Stamats implements the “Label Test” to help institutions make helpful, audience-friendly wording choices. When testing these words (at right), only 5% of responses across all respondents were correct. We used this test to help the college understand that internal, or technically “correct,” terms often are not understood by the target audience.

It isn’t about our opinion, it’s about tested designs that drive significant enrollment results for our clients.

question: Competitor Analysis
answer:
Creative Assets/Competitive Analysis

To help us understand where the “white space” exists for [Client Name], we will review the key branding elements of the [Client Name]’s top direct and indirect competitors or peers. Our competitive assessment includes collecting available marketing materials and review of online, digital, and social properties. We will prepare a synopsis of each institution’s key communications ideas (positioning), topline messages, brand tone, personality (language), and visual expression.

Competitor Spend

The initial competitor analysis will be conducted using tools such as Spyfu, Similar Web, and [Client Name]'s historic search advertising data. Further, during keyword research the relative competitive environment of each targeted term will be assessed. This will present a baseline measurement of the present state of competition in the targeted geography.

Unfortunately, the competitive environment isn’t static. Advertisers raise and lower ad spend, new competitors enter while others leave, not to mention the varying pressures at the program level from too many or too few registered students. For these reasons, competitive analysis must be ongoing and timely enough to provide direction at a tactical level.

In digital advertising, one of the best tools for analyzing market competition is Google Ads itself. With extensive reporting at a granular level, Google Ads reports on many useful measurements such as:

Each advertiser’s share of the search market.

The frequency with which advertisers appear in the same search results.

How often an advertiser beats competitors with better ad position.

These and other indicators are found from campaign-level down to each keyword targeted.

The advertiser can also learn if they are competing against themselves. Are pay-per-click ads being served to searches where the advertiser already “owns” the top organic, non-paid links? The “Paid vs. Organic” report is also a good barometer for the content team, finding content gaps where ads are served, and the site hasn’t earned any organic links.

Competitor insights are an important part of each month’s performance report meeting.

Deliverables include:

Content Plan and Assessment, Content Template, Content Style Guide, Model Content, Content Development, Writing for the Web Training.

question: New Academic Program Demand Assessment
answer:
Introduction

Many institutions are launching new programs to boost enrollment. With a new academic program demand assessment, an external third-party view of the situation is provided to guide faculty members and committees with robust data on expected cohort sizes, volume and growth trends, occupational outlook, hiring trends and employer expectations. National data and at your institution’s regional or core recruitment level, combined with insightful competitor reviews of successful programs can help your institution find the winning strategy in an already crowded market. Institutions find new academic program assessments helpful for strategic planning, shaping the new program focus and establishing key marketing components to maximize enrollment.

Discovery Meeting

We find it most effective to begin the process with an in-depth discussion of the new program currently being considered by [Client Name] leadership and faculty members. Program ideas may come from perceived needs in the labor market, emerging trends in student demand, faculty interests/areas of expertise, and/or priorities among senior leadership. Furthermore, in this meeting we want to understand:

Your internal vetting process and team members associated with the new program development

Your goals, desired outcomes and timeline for the project

Past new program launches

Gather institutional data, reports, program description, curriculum direction (if available) or other ideas for the desired program

Identify perceived competitors or aspirational programs for the desired program

This ensures that the research to follow covers program options and direction from your core leadership team.

Assessment

Data insights you can expect from an academic demand assessment are:

Conferral/Enrollment expectation – based on 3-5 years of data (by program, secondary data compiled from IPEDS), the trend, and average cohort size.

Number of institutions offering program and trend in number (national, core market)

Modality trend and cohort size differences

Labor Market Demand Trends at the national and core market level delivered are typically:

Occupation projections for SOC titles directly related to your program (national, core market

Job posting volume and trend related to your program (national, core market)

Top employers (national, core market)

Top job titles (national, core market)

Top occupations by SOC codes (national, core market)

Top skills advertised in job postings

Top geographic locations

Expected salaries and education for professionals in the field

Searching association, accreditation, or other publicly available sources can supplement the student demand and labor demand trends noted above. The availability of this data is determined on a case-by-case basis.

Competitor benchmarking is also conducted to understand how select providers are offering and performing. Data insights you can expect for key competitors are:

Program name, department – titles matter for SEO search

Delivery (mode, start terms, convenience, time to deliver, locations, etc…)

Cost, aid if appropriate

Curriculum overview

Concentrations, tracks offered

Admission requirements

Hands-on experiences or offerings associated to the degree

Career outcomes suggested

Key messages

Accreditation

Competitor profiling is crucial to understanding how your program will stand up. Past projects often point to re-examining the institution’s plan for delivery, price, marketing, concentrations, hands-on experiential offering, career aspirations, capstone requirements, prerequisites, accreditation standards, key partnerships.

Deliverable

[Client Name] will receive a summary report of all findings in PowerPoint with commentary on potential success, program considerations that will lead to attracting students.  Graphics and tables are displayed in an easy-to-read format and include insightful commentary of the results. All data sources are provided and accompanying Excel spreadsheets delivered for additional exploration if necessary.

Timeline

After our initial kick off Discovery meeting, the entire project can generally be completed in 4-5 weeks.

question: Campaign Landing Pages
answer:
The landing page is where the conversion happens. That is why connecting the user intent with the landing page is an important part of the overall enrollment journey. Stamats will design and develop a set of landing pages. Each campaign will most likely have its own page for a cohesive connection between the ad and the conversion.

question: Social Media, Web Content, and Email Review
answer:
Experience Mapping and Mystery Shopping

The overall journey is a key piece of the user experience. The Stamats team will evaluate user paths and conduct “mystery shopping” on your website and social media channels to identify overall journey experiences and review the prospective audience journey. This exercise will provide data-driven recommendations for updating your content and conversion points.

As part of your project, our Google-certified team will evaluate up to three journey paths and pages of the [Client Name]website. We will seek and surface trends within your data to guide the creation of your communications and marketing strategy and provide a baseline for performance. Stamats implements Google Analytics, heatmapping, keyword rankings, and other assessment tools to get a full picture.

Social Media Review

As part of your project, Stamats will review up to two social media channels—we strongly recommend LinkedIn as one such channel—to either help you decide which channels to use or help you better understand how you are doing on social media in terms of:

Frequency

Relevance

Findability

Tone/voice

Engagement

Conversion opportunities

Branding

Stamats will provide areas of opportunity, wins, and mockups of tangible recommendations that don't require coding. Additionally, we will provide examples of current social media experts in your field who are excelling at social media.

Email Content Review

When done right, email can become a highly effective communication tool for nearly every audience. As part of your project, Stamats strategists will evaluate up to three of your current emails or enewsletters for effectiveness, including:

Who the content is trying to reach

Whether included content is relevant, useful, appropriate, and substantial enough

Whether the content is conversion-centric or “doing its job”

Whether the layout/content strategy works for the content/audience

Based on our findings, we will offer recommendations to improve your content and keep or refine what is working. We will also provide a mockup that doesn’t require coding of how you might position your email or enewsletter content in the future.

question: Can automated email be added to the mix? Other technologies?
answer:
Automated email can take your campaign to another level. As a lead converts to an inquiry, the needs of the recruit change. Then, as they convert from an inquiry to an applicant and eventually matriculant, the needs change again from the top of the funnel.

As a lead, little is known about the person, limiting how to start or continue a conversation. More is learned at the inquiry stage, and you can begin to engage in information and questions about features/benefits of the interested program, cost to attend, and steps to apply.

At the point of application, students can present in three basic stages:

Application started but not submitted

Application submitted, admission file is incomplete

Application complete, decision made

There is also a secondary phase to the “application complete, decision made” that needs attention to ensure the student matriculates to the first day of class. During the application stages, communication is unique to the phase. For instance, the student who has not submitted their application does not need to know about supporting documents or steps to take to start class.

Email is a crucial tool to include beginning at the point of inquiry. At this stage, [Client Name]can provide relevant information to the student and position themselves as an expert in their field with key information about going to college and how to take the necessary steps. During this stage, the school can also seek to engage students to visit campus, schedule a meeting to learn more, and get personal help with steps to apply, fund their education, and attend. This two-fold approach of synchronizing email with services helps build rapport and relationships between students, family decision influencers, and your recruitment office.

These steps can expand and contract based on the target audience:

Some continuing education courses do not require all the steps for a traditional student seeking to attend.

Traditional students may need longer and lengthier communications to encourage taking next steps to matriculate.

Another technology that is becoming increasingly important with the death of third-party cookies is the availability of chatbots. Implementation of a chatbot to your homepage and other strategic pages will help you engage with potential students. Some of the key pages for communication and recruitment may include:

Admissions

Paying for college

Cost of attendance

Request for info

The purpose of the chatbot is to feed directly into the communication plan. At this level of engagement, inquiring students can learn about attending [Client Name] and engage by providing first-party consent for communications. Once consent is confirmed and basic contact information obtained, their data is funneled into a segmented communication sequence to continue the conversation and nurture them through application and enrollment.

question: A Recommendation for an In Place Redesign
answer:
Based on our initial review, we believe your site has a high likelihood of being a redesign in place. To confirm this recommendation, a backend review is required.
At no cost, Stamats is willing to attend a one hour back-end review of your [Client CMS] environment where your development team meets with our team. Based on that review, we believe we can confirm our recommendation.
Update In Place Redesign
If the free review confirms that an In Place Redesign is feasible, we recommend this approach. It allows your budget to focus on more strategic enhancements, such as improving analytics, optimizing content, enabling deeper personalization, and refining your site’s taxonomy.
Full Redesign
If an In Place Redesign isn’t viable, a full redesign would be necessary. This approach requires a greater investment in foundational elements such as site architecture, design framework, and technical infrastructure. While it offers a clean slate, it may limit the immediate focus on strategic enhancements, such as personalization, content writing, migration assistance, and advanced content strategy, until the core framework is fully rebuilt.
Update In Place Design Inclusions/ProsFull Redesign Inclusions/ProsGA4 Audit and Goals RecommendationsFull redesign for all pagesKey user journey audits and recommendationsFull content inventory and new IAContent Writing for 10 pagesFull website rebuild Writing for the WebMigration plan and sample pagesPage Layouts for 5 pages GovernanceRequires Client to manage contentPersonalization strategy and buildMany other features are optional above the budget
Based on the findings of the free review, and if feasible, we would recommend moving forward with an Update In Place Redesign.

question: What is your perspective on the demise of third-party cookies in digital marketing?
answer:
We see privacy laws as a response to consumer concerns—not a threat to advertising. As marketers, it's our responsibility to adapt with privacy-safe strategies that earn trust and loyalty through respectful, non-invasive tactics.
With the decline of retargeting and third-party cookies, higher ed marketers must rely on broader, smarter approaches—like contextual targeting and strong, first-party data strategies. While third-party data once played a role, it was rarely central to campaign success.
This shift is an opportunity to build more effective search and social strategies, grounded in first-party data and thoughtful audience engagement.

question: Encryption
answer:
All email communication is encrypted in Microsoft 365 by default. Microsoft uses Transport Layer Security (TLS) to encrypt the connection, or session, between two servers.
Data security for all SharePoint information is defined by Microsoft as Microsoft offers robust, multi-layered protection for data security in SharePoint Online. While BitLocker provides disk-level encryption of all data, per-file encryption ensures every change to a file is encrypted using a unique encryption key. Each encryption step uses Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) with 256-bit keys and is Federal Information Processing Standard (FIPS) 140-2 compliant.
Data stored on Windows servers or backup media is not encrypted.

question: Digital Campaign Discovery
answer:
Campaign Discovery Process
As part of our Discovery process, we will first review any recent research, planning, and strategic documents (e.g., marketing plans, audience research, media plans, media tracking and analysis, brand documents, historic advertising performance, enrollment plan, etc.).
A Stamats team consisting of your chief strategist, account manager, and digital media strategist will meet with you to discuss current opportunities and challenges as well as needs, expectations, and goals for the future. We will conduct facilitated discussions to develop a foundational understanding of [Client Name]'s value proposition and points of difference that resonate with your target audiences.
The campaign’s overall effectiveness will be based on a shared understanding of [Client Name]'s concrete goals and objectives and the challenges we may face as we strive to achieve them. Clearly defined goals, key performance indicators (KPIs), and a solid understanding of overall strategy is the first step to building both a strong relationship and campaign.
Marketing Audit
The audit phase builds a foundation for campaign strategy. Having a realistic view of your current and past outreach efforts—in terms of both quantity and quality—helps us establish a baseline for strategic development and helps us make informed decisions. Stamats will meet with your team to review historical efforts and fully understand your needs, goals, and processes.
Audience Definition
Personas are developed to provide context to motivations and the user journey. They also provide context for traditional and digital ad placements, search engine key words, and content “offers.” It is also critical that these audiences are addressable—that we can identify where and how to find them.
Messaging
With your audience and goals defined, and based upon your brand guidelines, we will create a messaging matrix to specify how you present your message to each audience.
Audience Intelligence
Beginning with a thorough understanding of your audience and their needs, we work together with your team to establish short- and long-term goals based on defined KPIs and measurements of success. As a recognized leader in higher education marketing, Stamats is an expert in reaching higher ed's important audience demographic (ages 18-44). Further, as one of the premier marketing organizations in the US for higher education marketing, Stamats has extensive capabilities in audience intelligence through our dedicated research team.
Understanding the demographics, psychographics, and behaviors of the target audiences are key to maximizing the effectiveness of brand marketing and lead generation media. We have access to over $2 million in annual research subscriptions that we leverage for our clients to provide a thoughtful media approach that will help accomplish their business goals. We can analyze the audience we want to reach in specific markets and determine the proper budgets to hit your targets and make an impact.
User Experience (UX)
Search engines read user signals to recommend sites with good UX. If your UX is subpar, SEO rankings suffer. We may look for and implement improvements that will make your site easier to navigate and view, including a review of colors, images and video, content, call-to-action placement, etc. Over 55% of website traffic comes from mobile devices, making mobile UX critical to your success. Our digital experiences will be optimized for mobile.
Strategic Brief
The Discovery deliverable is a strategic brief, outlining our understanding of goals, objectives, budgets, timelines, KPIs, messaging, and accountabilities. Stamats recommends defining and tracking KPIs by audience. Our KPI tracker will identify the specific target to which the KPI applies. The brief also contains the Campaign Execution Plan (CEP) that requires [Client Name]input as well, including who on the team is responsible for what activities.

question: First-Party and Third-Party Data
answer:
Stamats prioritizes first-party data for targeting. When third-party data is used, we’re transparent—explaining how it's sourced and the implications. For instance, programmatic platforms often rely on third-party demographic and interest lists, which we clearly outline when recommended.
With browsers like Safari and Firefox blocking third-party cookies, up to 50% of web traffic may be unreachable via traditional retargeting. This shift limits the effectiveness of cookie-based tactics.
When using first-party data, Stamats ensures compliance with privacy standards. Data is hashed for anonymity, and we only use platforms that delete lists post-matching—such as Google’s 'similar audience' tools.

question: General SEM Campaign
answer:
Search campaigns are most effective when targeting prospects ready to commit—but even then, users often conduct 10+ searches over several days. Staying visible in search results during this phase is crucial.
While many agencies rely passively on Google Ads' built-in AI, Stamats takes a hands-on approach. We guide machine learning with smart strategy, up-to-date campaign structures, and accurate data inputs—getting better results, faster.
We don’t rely on outdated methods or “set it and forget it” AI. Instead, we actively manage and optimize campaigns to outperform standard agency results.

question: Give an example of how you analyzed data to yield actionable insights for a client and how you leveraged those insights to further the client’s goals.
answer:
We can best address this question with an example—from one of our long-term clients Pima Community College.
When we launched our email campaigns in partnership with the Pima marketing department in 2021, we noticed a trend within the campaign actions—email recipients were clicking the “apply” link. This trend is particularly noteworthy, as the largest portion of the list is from leads. Leads are a touchy audience, as contacting them is equivalent to making a cold call.
Through this identified trend in data, we made an intentional design to highlight the link to apply, placing it front-and-center in all emails for leads and inquiries. This decision resulted in Pima Community College reporting they were ahead in app count in late fall of 2021 YTD. By the end of the recruitment year, we had yielded more than 700 clicks to apply from email campaigns.
This data provided actionable insights for Pima recruitment efforts; the Pima team could follow up personally and assist students with completing the application process. The second year, with additional Pima staff to track results, recruitment could claim more than 45% yield of prospects who have completed the application from the click to apply list, and 37% of the 45% were registered for class. These are excellent results, considering the audience was mostly cold outreach from the beginning.

question: Google Business Profile Audit and Listings Management
answer:
Accurate listings on Google and local directories are essential for reaching prospective students and stakeholders. Incorrect contact information can create frustration and harm credibility.
Stamats offers a proven approach to ensure your listings are consistent and visible across platforms like Google, Yelp, and Niche—which often appear above your main site in search results. We provide clear recommendations and strategies for ongoing updates, tailored to your school's needs.

question: Graphic Design
answer:
Through an integrated, multichannel brand storytelling program, you will experience an increase in prospect engagement and develop a richer, more conversion-centric marketing program. Our creative team includes talented designers and writers who specialize in several fields: institutional identity work (logos, brand identity), tactical and asset deliverables (content, print, ads), and website/digital (full and partial websites, landing pages, related ads).
​Stamats creates engaging and innovative conversion-centered ads, infographics, and other media to garner attention and drive interest. Stamats offers photography and videography (beyond the basic creation of YouTube pre-roll ads). Our team conceives, writes, and designs across the full spectrum of platforms, from stunning print layouts and graphics to trendy (and accessible) digital creative. In all instances, the emphasis is on conversion: moving the prospect from point A to point B along their decision-making journey from early awareness to application and enrollment.
Our work is a finely tuned balance of research and aspiration/creativity. We begin by working with you to mine the data and research you’ve already collected, driving to understand what is meaningful and relevant against the goals of the project at hand. When we have a firm grasp of data, we then move forward to design. Here’s where we work with you to craft the optimal experience, look, and feel for communication. We begin by defining the simplest and clearest message for the audience and brand.
Once we have clearly defined a layout, we conceive the graphic elements that make the experience warm, comfortable, and something they want to repeat. Whatever form the piece takes, it should embody the emotional experience you want to impart upon the intended audience.

question: Guerilla Marketing
answer:
Our approach to guerilla marketing is to have an aware team. Aware of your goals and unique opportunities. There is no set playbook or strategy—you need to be alert and ready when an event occurs.
Recently, our client, Morehead State University, deployed their Lunar IceCube on Artemis I. Social posts and integration into their top space program was part of the media strategy.
We partnered with MedStar Washington Hospital Center to create a “how to safely watch the solar eclipse” story for a once-in-decades total eclipse. We posted it several days prior to the event, capitalizing on unconventional traffic sources, such as schoolteachers and civic organizations. Following the eclipse, the hospital posted social media pictures of staff safely watching the eclipse following the advice in the article. The story was one of the top-viewed blogs that year and positioned the hospital as a warm resource for lifestyle educational content.
For college and university events, a simple strategy that has worked well is to create individual Facebook events to generate free visibility by those who mark attending or interested.

question: Hosting Options - WordPress

question: Guided Pathways Redesign
answer:
Pathways don't just serve to inspire future students. With credentials and micro-credentials, students as well as established professionals can find opportunities to improve their skills and advance. With career advancement comes economic growth for your community and better futures for your students and neighbors.
Our first step for the Guided Pathways redesign will be to storyboard the journey. Students, both future and current, can see potential paths for themselves as they consider the variety of careers and upskilling in their chosen field.
Next, we'll create an information design that makes it easier to follow the pathways and consume the information for each. This step will include a heavy edit of the existing content for a sample pathway.
Then we'll move from information design to visual design. After approvals of the visual design, we will build a sample in WordPress using your existing components; we will also design an additional component if needed.
Price for Pathways Redesign: $14,900

answer:

question: How do you define the line between support requests covered in the initial implementation of your CMS versus what is post-launch support?
answer:

question: How do you mesh paid search/paid social and digital display to optimize campaign performance?
answer:
An effective strategy targets prospects at every stage of their decision-making journey—recognizing that this path is often non-linear. That’s why a strong digital campaign must be present across the entire funnel.
Display ads build top-of-funnel awareness. Paid Social bridges awareness and action by reinforcing brand familiarity. Paid Search captures high-intent users ready to act.
Success requires ongoing attention and adaptability. Campaigns must be monitored regularly, with adjustments based on real-time behavior, search trends, and audience data. When one team oversees all tactics, insights are applied quickly—for example, shifting budget from slowing Paid Social to high-performing Paid Search to maintain momentum.

question: How do you track and prioritize accessibility issues?
answer:
Accessibility issues are logged in our bug tracking system with an “accessibility” tag. Any compliance issue found on a live production page is marked “high priority.”
All testing follows WCAG 2.2 AA standards. Issues within A–AA levels are addressed and prioritized based on their impact to users and client goals. High-priority fixes are resolved first to ensure a smooth launch, with lower-priority items handled in subsequent tiers.

question: How do you use analytics and user data in discovery?
answer:
Analytics-Driven Recommendations
As part of Discovery, Stamats’ Google-certified team analyzes user behavior, journey paths, and performance trends using tools like Google Analytics, heatmaps, and keyword rankings. Findings are shared with the [Client Name] team in the Discovery Audit Presentation to inform strategy and benchmark performance.

question: How is your company preparing to leverage emerging technologies?
answer:
Stamats takes a proactive approach to emerging technologies, ensuring our strategies stay current and our clients benefit from the latest advancements in digital marketing.
We continuously update client accounts by applying best practices that leverage artificial intelligence (AI) and machine learning for improved outcomes. As AI-powered campaign types become standard across advertising platforms, Stamats actively documents the required assets and techniques that contribute to their success. More than just adopting AI, we focus on identifying and using the specific controls and levers that help us guide these technologies to achieve targeted results.
As a Google Premier Partner, Stamats gains early access to beta features within the Google ecosystem. This advantage has enabled our clients to benefit from under-the-hood AI bidding strategies, new campaign formats like responsive display ads, and upgraded performance reporting tools—well before they’re widely available.
After a thorough evaluation, Stamats selected Optmyzr as our campaign management and optimization platform. Optmyzr enables us to apply automation, access AI-powered insights, and generate in-depth reporting. By automating math-based, repetitive optimizations, we free up our team’s time to focus on applying strategic innovations and emerging AI tools where they matter most.
One example: While AI still cannot reliably write high-quality ad copy, it excels at comparative analysis. Stamats has developed a custom AI agent that compares a client’s program content with that of three to four competitors. This gap analysis informs stronger positioning, allowing us to identify key differentiators and reduce the inefficiencies often associated with launching campaigns without baseline insight.
When Google announced the transition from Universal Analytics to GA4 in 2022, Stamats acted immediately—implementing GA4 for our clients a full year before the official switchover. This gave them early access to new tracking capabilities and ensured uninterrupted data collection during the change.
Beyond advertising, our commitment to innovation extends to websites and email. Stamats develops digital assets that meet WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility standards. This supports users with visual or cognitive challenges and aligns with evolving technologies like Alexa and other voice assistants, ensuring content is accessible in both traditional and voice-activated formats.
Finally, we optimize all websites for both traditional and voice search by focusing on user intent and crafting content that directly answers the types of questions users ask, whether typed or spoken.

question: How Proposed Redesign Services Will Achieve Result Requirements: Attract, Inform, and Engage Audiences
answer:
Attract, inform, and engage prospective students, employees, visitors, alumni and donors with micro interactions, immersive storytelling, video, animations, and other engaging type features.
We view a university website as a central tool in driving demand—not just an information source. It should guide visitors through a journey of interest, engagement, application, enrollment, and ultimately, retention. Our design approach focuses on delivering the right content at the right time to encourage conversion.
Unlike many firms, Stamats treats the website as the centerpiece of a broader strategy—supporting admissions, advancement, and community engagement through intentional, audience-focused experiences. It should generate inquiries, foster micro-conversions, and support both external goals and internal needs like student satisfaction and retention.
Throughout the project, we’ll help [Client Name] stay focused on the site’s core public-facing purpose while distinguishing it from internal intranet functions.

question: How to Test PDFs for Accessibility
answer:
PDFs are a stable but outdated format that can clutter your website and frustrate users. While easy for internal teams to upload, they often hinder usability and lack flexibility.
Our workshop helps you assess whether a PDF should stay, be converted to web content, or be removed. Using metrics like content lifespan, traffic, strategic value, and policy, we’ll guide you in making informed decisions—transforming your site into a more user-friendly, effective experience.

question: Implementation of Marketing and Communications Plan
answer:
We start as your partner by researching and building a smart, strategic plan. From there, we take an agile approach—continually monitoring and optimizing for performance and outcomes.
We identify where demand lives—whether we're creating it or capturing it—and ensure both work together to drive prospects through the funnel.
Our team crafts and tests compelling messages, using AI and real-time data to optimize calls-to-action as audiences move through the journey.
Timing matters. We align strategies with your cohort calendar, ramping up investment during peak periods and maintaining steady momentum in between.
The full experience counts. We mystery shop multiple times, even beyond our contracted scope, to validate execution and uncover ways to reduce drop-off and boost engagement.
We rely on continuous analysis to guide decisions. Our Google-certified analysts ensure deep integration between Google Ads and GA4, bridging the gap left by third-party cookies and surfacing conversion path insights to enhance both campaigns and site experience.

question: Include the retention rate of employees over the last ten years.
answer:
10 Year Retention Rate
Stamats has had a retention rate of 30%.
Our average turnover rate has been less than 30% since the Covid period. Pre-Covid, our turnover rate was 16-20%. This also includes retirements, which several occurred with Covid. We have 37% of employees with over 10 years at Stamats.

question: Information Architecture - Alternate
answer:
Our strategy is to deliver the best UX to meet your goals. We set up information architecture to elevate the content that users seek (so they don’t have to dig) and create natural connections for their visit. We create journeys through the architecture to meet your recruitment (and other) goals. Visual design continues this strategic guidance of the user’s attention and answers some of the harder questions—Do I belong here? Is [Client Name]the right college/university for me? Am I right for college?
Our sitemap proposes new navigation and directory structures for your site, starting from the homepage and extending up to four levels deep. The map visualizes menus, key page content, and conversion paths measurable in Google Analytics.
The sitemap identifies gaps, redundancies, or failures in current structures and offers new solutions. We’ll review our recommendations with you, address feedback, and deliver two rounds of revisions.

question: Information Design and Visual Design
answer:
Our strategy is to deliver the best UX to meet the college’s/university's goals. We set up information architecture to elevate the content that users seek, so they don’t have to dig. We create journeys through the architecture to meet your recruitment (or other) goals. Visual design continues this strategic guidance of the user’s attention and answers some of the harder questions—Do I belong here? Is this college the right one for me? Am I right for college?
Our approach is similar across web disciplines: shape the user’s experience. We integrate it through the people. Your experienced, digital project manager conducts the band throughout the project. The information architect and designer both participate in discovery. The digital strategy team documents decisions to make smoother handoffs from one discipline to the next.

question: Integrating/Coordinating SEO and SEM
answer:

question: Migration Support Services
answer:
Stamats has provided migration services for many of our college and university clients. The type and amount of migration depends first on the quality of your current content. Migrating content that does not meet accessibility, readability, tone, or storytelling is a common mistake. Our approach and strategy are based on timelines, client staff resources (personnel and budget), and the amount/type of new or updated content.
Migrating content is more than just pasting current content; it strategically aligns the content to the design elements. The UNCW example on the right illustrates an As-Is page where the content did not change but was moved into flexible components for improved UX.
For all content, Stamats creates a mapping plan to show what types of content will migrate to specific components to create consistency and the best UX experience possible with easy-to-consume content.
