question: Migration Timelines
answer:
Stamats Protects Your Priority Timelines
The agency you select will ultimately reflect upon your reputation, both inside your organization and externally. We understand this and take this responsibility very seriously. Your deadlines are our deadlines. We do not let our clients down.
In the past year, two schools—who both opted for lower-cost bids over Stamats for their migration projects—came back to Stamats in urgent situations. One school asked us to complete the work of their original vendor, while the other asked us to completely redo the project from scratch.
Even though both projects specified required deadlines in their RFPs, they missed those deadlines by more than a year. Given the significant delays, Stamats was asked to migrate over 1,000 pages in less than two months. We delivered all pages ahead of schedule, exceeding the client’s expectations. We also provided additional services, such as custom content for brand-new pages, a redirect plan, and analytics optimization.
Stamats meets ALL migration timelines, including incredibly tight deadlines.
Our Content Migration Expertise is Unmatched
We have one of the most experienced migration teams in the business. Our team has migrated tens of thousands of pages and has been met with virtually every scenario, technically and strategically. No matter how complex your requirements may seem, they will not be a “first” for us.
A successful complex migration project requires a powerful process. Our agile process has been tested and fine-tuned through many project types and technologies. Our team, led by two certified ScrumMasters, understands how to break down work to efficiently move pages through the system.
First, we create a mapping plan to ensure consistency across the project. Experience tells us that, given the complexity of your pages and content authors, there may be inconsistencies in your content. When migrating the content, we will follow a tracking process to correct inconsistencies across pages.
Our hiring process focuses on individuals who are highly skilled in layouts, advanced Microsoft Word, and quality assurance. This is a unique set of core capacities! We have seen evidence in dozens of sunshine reports that our competitors often opt for outsourced technical migrators who lack content best practices expertise and the visual understanding of a holistic page experience.
The Stamats migration team is trained in Drupal, Sitecore, WordPress, Cascade, and Omni. Our development team also has experience and expertise with Divi, Gutenberg, PublishPress, and Drupal, and in the last year, we have launched complex sites on Divi and Drupal.
Your Stamats team will include a dedicated Content Project Manager. Your PM has led dozens of migrations projects. Again, your project will not be a training ground for our team.

question: More Reasons to Embrace Cascade: Streamlined Content Management and Workflows
answer:
Cascade CMS is purpose-built to help higher education institutions streamline content management and enforce institution-wide web governance. With Cascade, you can create tailored user interfaces and permissions for each type of contributor, from faculty editors to members of your marketing team, ensuring everyone has access to the right sites, folders, content fields, and styling tools appropriate to their role.
Unlike many CMS platforms, Cascade allows you to define custom groups and roles with highly granular permissions, even down to specific options in the WYSIWYG editor. This ensures that governance policies are enforced consistently, no matter how many users or websites you manage.
Content is managed through structured fields, enabling standardized content entry, easier quality control, and effortless content reuse across departments, microsites, and marketing channels. These structured content models support university-wide branding and accessibility guidelines while reducing the risk of off-brand or outdated content.
Custom workflows in Cascade CMS help you enforce review and approval processes, whether you’re looking for simple one-step approvals or complex multi-step workflows with conditional routing. Every step in the process is tracked and time-stamped, providing a clear audit trail to support compliance and transparency.
Cascade allows you to keep unlimited versions for each page, so you can see what each page looked like at any time. You can also compare each version to the previous one and roll back to any version with just one click.
Administrators benefit from built-in dashboards that surface content freshness, workflow status, and user activity, giving them real-time visibility into the health of their digital ecosystem.
One recently added feature, the Content Inventory Report, lets you get a complete snapshot of your content assets and generate action items to improve content quality, governance, and collaboration.

question: Optimized Campaigns = Reduced Ad Cost
answer:
Google is in the business of delivering the right content to the user. As part of this core philosophy, Google rewards campaigns that are optimized for the intent of the user. Gone are the days of being able to stuff keywords or buy keywords that are not a match to your content.
It is a known fact that optimized campaigns pay less per ad than campaigns that are not highly managed. In most cases, the cost savings from optimizing a campaign can pay for the management costs. Google rewards investing in strategy and creating relevant user journeys.
In an example of a previous campaign, the Stamats team was able to improve the click-through rate (CTR) significantly month-to-month through increasingly optimized ads and an improved landing page experience. This improved performance led to a 50% reduction in ad costs and breakeven at after just two months! All this while maintaining or exceeding the conversions.

question: Personalization - Generic
answer:
The key to a successful personalization experience starts with understanding the user journey and making sure touchpoints are connected all the way through the current visit as well as the many repeat visits.
In general, personalization is a series of if, then statements that guide a user through a defined path. Along the path, the user can opt out of the journey by navigating to alternative content. With any strategy, having options for the user to see additional content is key. Personalization should not hide content as much as it should prioritize content.
When launching personalization, you are committing to ongoing monitoring and adjustment. While personalization provides rich data and insights into user behavior, strategies must be reviewed regularly to make sure users are not struggling along the path or are simply not interested in the nurture stream created. This commitment to investing not only upfront but throughout the year creates a connected experience for the user.

question: Personas and User Journeys
answer:
Stamats will lead a persona workshop with the [Client Name] team where we will develop a set of meaningful audience segments and sketch out each audience’s user journey. We will use insight from other campus focus groups and additional background research as we develop robust and detailed personas for each segment.
For each persona, the conversion differentiators, content conversion tactics, and key conversion steps are defined. Personas will serve as a guide for the team as they develop content and evaluate the site experience.
By diving into intentions, motivations, tasks, worries, and aspirations of these characters, we deepen our understanding of target audiences. Personas guide the internal content team by building empathy for your users. For the strategy team, personas guide stronger journey paths—more persuasive calls to action, a more complete response to user tasks, and clearer goals and KPIs.
Higher ed websites often fail because they are organized around internal structures or internal biases. “That is not how I use the site.” “Students will never be able to find that content.” “That won’t work for our department.” “We all call the program SASPCTC, so that’s what the menu should say. The full name is too long, and we can’t use anything but the formal name or abbreviation.”
Personas counter this internal bias and can be a key part of changing your campus approach to the website, far beyond content and calls-to-action.
Internal thinking diverts even the best intentions to a compromised user experience or diluted governance plan. Our key strength is our Change Management Methodology. We will guide stakeholders into understanding the value, changing mindsets on content structure, and preparing them to support and defend a user-centric website.
How does the vendor plan to present and report on the target personas?
Initial personas are shared with the [Client Name] team for discussion and review. Personas will be finalized based on [Client Name]'s feedback and then approved. From there, personas are incorporated into the overall strategy, information architecture, and content.

question: Writing Press Releases as Engaging News Stories
answer:
When news breaks, speed and accuracy matter. With push alerts and social media updates everywhere, your organization must be a trusted, timely source that informs and reassures.
Even with a small team, a Rapid News Response plan can help you deliver key messages efficiently—and show ROI. This session teaches your team how to maximize one piece of content across multiple channels, like turning a blog post into social updates and a quick website notice.
We'll help you move fast, stay accurate, and keep your audience informed.

question: Program Finder with Interactive Search or Program Workshop with Static Layout
answer:
We believe our experience with Program Finders would greatly benefit [Client Name]’s various prospective student audiences for the new site. Our interactive finder allows students to identify academic programs that serve their goals. Users can search by keywords, filter by various categories (such as degree/credential type, interests, campus location, delivery mode, outcomes/careers, or other criteria), and receive a customized list of matching programs.
If you choose to not build a Program Finder, a scaled-down version Program Workshop will focus on the overall strategy, user experience, and user journey—both on the page and into the programs. This strategy will include how to address Hawkeye Community College’s array of associate and certificate programs. Our ongoing research finds that prospective students of all types first go to academic program information.
An interactive program finder engages users and quickly highlights options that fit their interests.

question: Program Specific Advertising
answer:
Program advertising picks up mid-funnel where brand campaigns leave off. While the discovery process is similar, the focus shifts to specific program strengths and competitive advantages—one program at a time. It's not enough to say “We offer Accounting Degrees”; we need compelling messaging that clearly communicates [Client Name]’s unique value.
During discovery, we go beyond basic questions about program capacity and start dates. We’ll dig into graduation rates, job placement, internships, student clubs, and other differentiators that resonate with prospective students.
Each program campaign is guided by a Campaign Execution Plan (CEP), outlining roles, budget, timeline, audience, calls to action, and key value points. The CEP evolves through discovery, becoming the foundation for targeted, high-impact messaging and media strategy.

question: Project Challenges
answer:
Everyone loves a good story, especially when it’s a story of success. At Stamats, we have a lot of them—stories of challenges being overcome, of great opportunities being seized, of organizational goals and aspirations being exceeded, and, quite simply, of compelling storytelling.
Of course, projects—even successful ones—don’t always go to plan. But, we can handle that too. Challenges happen. We’ve found they tend to fall into two areas: timeline and organizational buy-in (change management).
Timeline
Our robust and proactive project management keeps the timeline always in view and adapting to events. However, sometimes clients (especially with large or dispersed teams) need longer review windows at various points than the timeline anticipated. We respond agilely—working forward with any non-contentious points and providing support to speed your review. We do need open and ongoing communication from [Client Name]'s team lead throughout the project.
Change Management
A redesign is about more than a new look or a new CMS. All the people, teams, and processes behind the site need to recalibrate and adjust. Or sometimes change radically. We, too, are not just about the new look. We understand and incorporate change management into our discovery, our reporting, and our planning.

question: Project Management Approach - General
answer:
Because Stamats is a full-service firm, we take responsibility for all work. We manage the project from inception to delivery of results and assets. As such, quality control remains in our hands. We develop the timetable and interim deadlines together, and the project budget is known and unchanging (unless we both agree to an adjustment reflective of scope change) from the price quoted in the contract. Our clients attest to efficient and on-time engagements attached to their respective and documented budgets.
[IF WEBSITE/DIGITAL ENGAGEMENT Whatever the partnership includes—from analytics to web graphic/UX design, to user interface design and web application development, IT systems integration, and other strategies and tactics—our team is prepared and experienced to manage and deliver the work.]
We assign team members who offer you experience and expertise. Our team has decades of collective strategy, planning, and qualitative and quantitative research experience developing sound research methodologies and delivering actionable insights for higher education institutions.
We maintain a collaborative partnership with your team throughout the project. We rely on a proven, multistep process that includes continual communication between the Stamats team and yours. During each step, your team will have the opportunity to review our work and make decisions about the best way to move forward. Maintaining a close relationship among all team members helps ensure our work is in sync with your goals.
We understand that success is built upon research and strategy. Your success is our success. The core of Stamats work is built on qualitative and quantitative research that clarifies your position, identifies strategic opportunities, provides insights about how your target audiences will respond, and drives an informed strategy.
We continually monitor project status. Up front, our team provides detailed timelines that clearly and accurately represent each task in the project, who is responsible for completing each task (including members of your project team), what deliverables are required, and what key dates and milestones need to be met. Throughout the engagement, we conduct internal status meetings and provide regular project status updates with your team.
We use Basecamp as our workflow and project management solution. Basecamp software allows us to remain in touch with each other and with our clients throughout each project phase. We rely on Basecamp for day-to-day status updates and for collaboration and document sharing.
Finally, our chief innovation officer, and head of our PM team, is a certified Project Management Professional.

question: Project Management, Reporting, Client Service & Support
answer:
A personnel plan that describes the kind of supports and access that are guaranteed to [Client Name]for this volume of a project.
At Stamats, great service starts with understanding your goals, challenges, and opportunities. We don’t take a one-size-fits-all approach—our solutions are tailored to where you are now, where you want to go, and what sets you apart.
As a full-service partner, we manage projects from start to finish, handling all logistics, timelines, and quality control. We work with you to define scope, set a fixed budget, and assign a team of experienced professionals with deep expertise in strategy, creative, and implementation.
Our process is collaborative and transparent. We emphasize open communication, ongoing updates, and clear roles through tools like Basecamp, Zoom, and Teams. You’ll always know what’s happening, what’s next, and who’s responsible.
Every decision is grounded in research and data, ensuring informed strategy and measurable results. We track progress with detailed timelines and regular status reports, and we respond quickly to questions or concerns through a dedicated point of contact.
Stamats brings a core team approach—senior leaders in strategy, creative, and project management work closely with discipline experts to deliver smart, integrated solutions. Our goal: to help you succeed through better ideas, better execution, and better results.
Finally, our chief innovation officer, and head of our PM team, is a certified Project Management Professional.

question: Proposed Project Team
answer:
Your Stamats team is anchored by the project manager who is the stable pivot point from kickoff to wrap. Your ever involved project sponsor spearheads the relationship, keeping an eye on every aspect.
All projects include project sponsor, senior account manager, digital sponsor, and account specialist. They remain active throughout the project. We bring higher ed, brand, research, digital, and Google-certified experience to your challenges.
As we move and adjust to the market and your needs, specialists may join at different times for such activities as content review, video editing, blog recommendations, etc.

question: Provide details about the reporting dashboard you will use, including how it integrates with existing systems and what specific metrics will be tracked.
answer:
Traditional print metrics (e.g., postcards, flyers, newsletters) can be tracked via unique QR codes linked to specific actions and added to our dashboard.
Email performance—like open and click-through rates—can also be included if data is shared.
To measure campaign effectiveness, we can conduct a pre- and post-campaign brand study assessing awareness, consideration, intent, and likelihood to recommend.
We recommend tracking monthly inquiries and applicants overall and by program to evaluate ROI throughout the campaign.
For traditional media (TV, radio, billboards, transit), impression estimates will be provided by media vendors.

question: QuickStart Project Approach & Timeline
answer:
QuickStartProject Approach & Timeline
We work on an agile methodology to allow space for new ideas, occasional crisis management, and opportunities that arise. Biweekly or weekly status meetings will allow us to adjust timelines as needed. Following is an example timeline—we will provide a personalized timeline for Mid at the start of your project.
Week 1: Define Campaign Parameters
Through our QuickStart approach, Stamats and [Client Name]will work through planning parameters in the form of a media brief for new campaign(s) inclusive of the goals, KPI, target audiences, key markets, timing, budgets, and creative assets available. We will review the media brief together to ensure all initial questions are answered and all parties are aligned on the campaign’s media objective, primary goals, and KPIs so Stamats can begin to develop our multichannel campaign recommendation.
We will map out the geographic footprint and define the addressable targets of the campaign(s). Also, we will recommend the tactics, channels, vehicles, duration, reach, and frequency of each element and for the overall campaign. Finally, we will select the exact placement recommendations and provide a rationale for each recommendation to obtain approval.
Using the media brief and digital media plan as our guide, Stamats will begin developing a strategic plan. We will craft the plan based upon several tenets, including that it must:
- Be an efficient and effective optimization of [Client Name]’s resources.
- Be built around the way prospects consume media and engage with institutions.
- Conversion funnel developed around [Client Name]’s key academic enrollment periods.
- Create awareness, engagement, demand, and preference for the [Client Name]brand.
- Focus on key touchpoints of the engagement journey.
- Be trackable and measurable against specified goals and KPIs.
Week 2: Strategy, Budget Allocation, and Plan Development
Leveraging the information provided, as well as data and information from our powerful digital marketing toolbox, Stamats will recommend an appropriate budget allocation by audience, channel, device, etc. to address campaign goals.
Potential Media Distribution (Subject to change based on discussions and past performance data.)

With a reduced timeline to launch and limited media budget, our team would focus on producing branded display ads first, launching on DATE. This would allow us to create awareness in your targeted counties leading up to the winter enrollment period. Three sets of display ads will be utilized (one for each enrollment period). Retargeting website visitors with branded ads will also allow for an increase in display conversions.
Depending on video assets available for Stamats to edit and/or modify, we will also plan to introduce video ads on DATE. Should new or additional footage be required, Stamats will work with [Client Name]to contract with a videographer, delaying the video ads launch. These ads will drive awareness for your locations and brand.
Sample of Stamats-created animated display ad.
In tandem with the launch of display and video ad tactics, paid search (SEM) and social media ads will capture mid- and bottom-funnel interest and conversions. We traditionally see increased competition from retailers during holiday shopping months, so reducing ad spend in December and January will save budget for higher converting months. SEM campaign budgets will be significantly reduced (not paused completely) in September, December, and January to avoid common campaign restart windows (relearning conversion and targeting) that delay efficacy. SEM ads cannot afford to advertise many individual programs due to budget constraints and competition in-market. However, clustering programs into roughly three or four themes (e.g., health sciences, business, STEM, trades, etc.) will drive users to relevant campaign landing pages (CLPs) to fill out RFI forms. Stamats will partner with [Client Name]on CLP creation following best practices.
We don’t add markup costs to your ad spend. Unlike many agencies, Stamats will never mark-up click costs. We believe in a true partnership with transparency in reporting, so you will know exactly where your budget is going. You can view all costs and datapoints 24 hours a day via our real-time campaign performance dashboards.
Present Strategic Plan, Audience Targeting & Approach
Once the plan has been developed, Stamats will prepare a presentation that concisely and effectively walks you through the planning sequence, from defining the competitive atmosphere and developing the strategy to executing the solution and measuring performance.
Client Collaboration, Feedback & Approval
Your feedback and/or approval is the next step. This is a key area of collaboration. We want to be challenged and pushed (and we will push back!) to ensure all parties grant buy-in on execution. For your specific project, Stamats will partner with your team to craft a set of HTML5 display designs. We will work together to finalize a set for launch with expectations that creative will continually be A/B tested and adjusted. For video ads, our team of designers can save [Client Name]time and budget by revising and editing existing footage. At this time, a reporting cadence will also be established based on campaign needs and client wants.
QuickStart Launch: DATE
Stamats Finalizes Technical Setup & Prepares Campaign Launch
We will work with you to determine if conversion and retargeting pixels are firing properly to track the performance of the campaign, as well as conduct a final QA check for any additional creative assets needed to launch as scheduled.
QuickStart Launch: DATE
Launch, Measure, Optimize, Repeat
After the campaign is launched, our team will intensely monitor campaigns during the initial 48 hours to ensure ads are being served and reach your intended audiences. We move to normal daily campaign reviews and optimizations thereafter, while additional campaigns may also launch throughout the duration of the engagement and require additional planning and strategy sessions.
Reporting will be provided based on the cadence decided in Step 4. Throughout the duration of the campaign, we will provide insights and optimizations to ensure the campaign goals and KPIs are exceeded. Additionally, a real-time 24/7 campaign digital dashboard will be developed so that your team has quick and easy access to ad performance metrics. It must be tied to key brand engagement and conversion goals as well as [Client Name]’s strategic direction.
Sample of Stamats’ 45-point Optimization Checklist

question: Radio & TV Ads
answer:
Stamats follows a multi-step process that includes a collaborative partnership between our creative team and [Client Name]’s team. Stamats encourages [Client Name]to select the level of effort and depth of services provided by our team, based on the capabilities of your internal or other external resources. Therefore, as the first step, we will provide [Client Name]with detailed timelines that clearly and accurately represent each project task, who is responsible for completing each task, what deliverables are required, and what key dates and milestones need to be met.
As with everything we do, during each step in the process, you will have the opportunity to review our work and make decisions about the best way to move forward with the creative elements of the audio and/or video(s) to ensure that your goals to utilize them across multiple platforms are met. Maintaining close communication between all team members helps to ensure our work matches your goals. Working from the established creative concept, we will move into script writing and production planning.
Scriptwriting and Production Planning
In this phase, we expand the approved concept into a full script and pre-production storyboard. Stamats will create a detailed production schedule and draft the script, allowing for up to three rounds of revisions. Your approval is required before moving forward.
Once the script is approved, our team will develop a storyboard to match. If needed, we’ll revise it based on your feedback. At the end of this step, you’ll have a finalized script and storyboard, ready for full ad production.

question: ROI and KPIs
answer:
We are especially attentive to ROI and success measures. We run a KPIs workshop as part of our standard discovery process. We develop custom measures, suited to your goals for the website—applications, recruitment, SEO rankings, traffic volume, reputation-building, scroll depth, bounce rates, etc. Rather than setting up output-related measures (e.g., number of pages completed), we prefer measures that align with your institutional goals for the website.
We often pair certain questions with their appropriate measures, such as content engagement with scroll depth or application starts with heatmaps of key admissions pages.

question: Server Hosting/Recovery Provider
answer:

question: Stakeholder & Executive Reporting
answer:
Your stakeholders are busy, and they don’t want every minute detail in reporting. They want answers to the big questions. Our experts deliver your successes succinctly in ways that provide maximum impact—and effectively highlight your team’s hard work.
Stamats strategists are internationally recognized storytellers and presenters, regularly presenting at industry conferences and in high-stakes executive leadership meetings.
In our presentations, we’ll provide your stakeholders with data-driven stories in a defined context with granular data available at a moment’s notice.

question: Site Launch and Support: Assessment Plan
answer:
We believe every new site needs an assessment plan as a check on project success and a promise to campus stakeholders. Pledging to track issues, test solutions, and measure the site’s performance against goals established up front can help assuage concerns and manage change.
Stamats can develop an assessment plan that helps you triage issues as they emerge, identify topics for longer-term monitoring or resolution, set monthly reporting goals, and schedule scaled assessments at three, six, and 12 months after launch.

question: Smart Social Partnerships and Paid Placements
answer:
Today’s social media thrives on collaboration. Platforms like Instagram and Facebook reward partnerships that boost visibility and engagement through simple, strategic tactics.
When appropriate, Stamats will recommend tagging and hashtag strategies to increase reach and relevance. Tagging organizations like Healthcare.gov or New Mexico HSD can help grow your audience and brand recognition organically.
Not every post needs paid support. Before recommending boosting or paid placement, we’ll ensure your visuals, messaging, calls-to-action, and landing pages work together seamlessly—so your investment drives real results.
Editorial Calendar
Creating and managing a centralized calendar helps your team and Stamats stay on track. As part of your scope of work, Stamats will update and manage your content calendar based on your business goals and scheduled content.
We’re happy to use any platform your team is used to, from software such as Monday or Asana to a simple Google Doc—we’ve done it all, and we’re flexible.
Deliverables: Strategy for social media outreach, engagement, and distribution of stories; editorial calendar for social story and organic post tracking.

question: Social Media Advertising
answer:
The integration of paid and organic social media can have an obvious synergistic effect by increasing awareness and share of voice. Beyond that, however, is the effect paid social can have on SEO by boosting relevant content to highly targeted audiences and driving more traffic, engagement, and links to your site—all of which are SEO factors that impact rankings.
Each social media platform has its own set of unique users. Facebook reaches parents, the number one influencer of high school students and their choice of college. Instagram, Tik Tok, and Twitch can engage higher education’s youngest audiences. LinkedIn is terrific to use when promoting graduate courses.
Did you know that when a college makes an organic post on Facebook/Instagram less than 30% of its followers get alerted to the new post? Paid ads on Facebook are the surest way to reach the bulk of your brand followers on the platform.
Stamats will work with your social media team to build a relationship between paid and organic advertising, including boosted posts, native advertising, and social content.

question: Stamats Accessibility Philosophy, Capabilities & Strengths
answer:
At Stamats, accessibility is core to creating inclusive digital experiences. We believe every user deserves equitable access to online content, regardless of how they navigate the web. Our approach is guided by the latest Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG 2.1 and 2.2), with a focus on Level AA compliance.
We take a comprehensive approach to accessibility evaluation, combining automated tools like the WAVE Evaluation Tool with manual testing and screen reader evaluation using NVDA, VoiceOver, and other assistive technologies. This ensures we identify both visible and structural accessibility barriers.
Our technical implementation relies on modern, standards-based web technologies—including semantic HTML5, WAI-ARIA (Accessible Rich Internet Applications), CSS, and JavaScript frameworks—optimized to work with contemporary browsers and assistive technologies. We also ensure proper use of ARIA roles, landmarks, and dynamic interface controls to enhance accessibility without compromising usability or performance.
Stamats also understands the limits of external systems such as PDFs, videos, or third-party platforms. We provide guidance and alternatives where full remediation isn’t feasible and help institutions develop strategies for long-term compliance.
Beyond audits, we offer hands-on remediation, training, and practical tools that empower teams to maintain accessibility. From captioning video to improving PDF usability and training staff on inclusive practices, we help clients build sustainable, accessible digital ecosystems that reflect their mission and values.
Maintaining an accessible site takes a commitment from leadership to allow for ongoing training, to hold firm in not posting PDFs/Videos/Charts that do not pass, and staying current with new changes. This governance is the most important part of any accessibility program. Otherwise the work done in the audit and remediation will be undone.

question: SuperTemplate Design and Use-Case Layouts
answer:
Next, we’ll apply the concept you’ve selected across the page types identified during Discovery. We will document guidelines for specific design and interactive features, ensuring you have the tools necessary to add even more page types as needed.
A key advantage of working with Stamats is our SuperTemplate approach. By leveraging a set of four flexible templates and shared components, we enable large enterprise sites to scale efficiently without the need for ongoing development. Content managers can easily create individualized pages using modular components that align with institutional branding while minimizing long-term costs.
To support consistent and strategic use, Stamats provides non-binding use-case layouts that guide component selection for various content types, such as department home pages. These guides help maintain visual consistency and prioritize key messaging across the site.
The SuperTemplate provides exceptional flexibility to support various content needs. In this example you can see four different options available.
A Note About “Mobile-First” Philosophy
Stamats takes an all-screens first approach to website development. So, yes, we do “mobile-first,” and we do “desktop-first” because we evaluate both during each stage of the design process so that one does not preclude the other. Without this integrated approach, the design experience would not be optimized for its native platform and therefore not integrate things such as hovers, swipes, image sizing, and background video. Contemporary web design must consider phones, desktops, and everything in-between from the start.
A new-to-Stamats Client recently commented.... “On our recent launch, the vendor did not build flexible templates and we were literally handcuffed. Stamats strategy is so flexible, and we have had them rebuild our new site so it can support our needs. We would have saved a lot of time and budget hiring Stamats originally.”

question: Timeline - Agile
answer:
Stamats follows an agile approach grounded in collaboration, iteration, and adaptability. Rather than relying on static documentation, we prefer to work with living examples that evolve based on your feedback. This flexible framework reduces risk, allowing us to add or adjust components—even late in the process—when real content or design reveals new needs.
Our overlapping process allows content and design to inform one another, building a stronger, more realistic foundation than a linear (waterfall) method. Agile also gives you space to share and refine designs internally, supporting a feedback-driven approach rather than rigid sign-offs.
We understand that changes are inevitable—even during content migration—and we’ve built flexibility into our components to accommodate evolving needs. That said, we’ll establish clear checkpoints in the project to help manage scope and ensure timelines and budgets stay on track. For example, once design begins, wireframe changes will pause, and functional requirements signal design approval.

UI/UX recommendations are incorporated into every website recommendation and deliverable we provide.

question: Upgrade Process
answer:
Upgrades to WordPress and PHP are made on a lower environment and tested prior to a production update.
Plugins should be updated at a minimum of once a month, if not automatically updating. A bi-weekly schedule is preferred. We recommend doing manual updates to allow these to be tested first on a lower environment.
Anytime a security or other major update is released, these should be considered major and done immediately.
A PHP upgrade may require updates if depreciated items were used in the code.
Many hosting providers will force a date when your system will be updated. Generally provide adequate time to prepare and methods for testing.

question: Website Redesign Risks for Clinical Trial
answer:
The main risk is normalizing clinical trial data across multiple providers. Stamats has extensive experience in data normalization and will create a standard input form while allowing for data exceptions. Another risk is the aggressive timeline. To mitigate this, both teams must stay responsive. Stamats will ensure clear communication and present information to simplify decision-making. If longer review periods are needed, we’ll adapt, progressing with non-contentious points and offering support to speed up the review process.

question: What is the worst disconnect you had with a client firm, and how did you resolve it?
answer:
Please know that Stamats is experienced in diplomatically navigating a wide range of issues and potential sticking points before they become problems. We work to avoid most challenges by clearly setting expectations and encouraging cross-departmental buy-in early. We will also strive to resolve issues efficiently by maintaining ongoing communication with our clients to ensure complete understanding of the process, strategic direction, and expected outcomes.
Challenges happen. We’ve found they tend to fall into three areas: understanding of internal operations, timeline, and campus buy-in (change management).
Internal Operations
One of the reasons we continue to do mystery shopping throughout the life of a campaign is there can be a disconnect between our client’s assumptions and actual operations. In one situation, the client thought they were sending prospects who engaged through a lead form an immediate text response and an email, followed by an email a week later and a phone call.
After concerns that the leads were not converting, our mystery shopping showed no communications were going out to those that completed the lead form. We immediately called a meeting and walked the client through the experience. Their CRM had somehow turned off all messaging and they weren’t aware. Our goal is to make sure the entire process is working together, regardless of whether it is in our scope.
Timeline
Our robust and proactive project management keeps the timeline always in view and adapting to events. However, clients with large or dispersed teams sometimes need longer review windows at various points than the timeline anticipated. We respond agilely, working forward with any non-contentious points and providing support to speed your review. This requires open and ongoing communication from your team lead throughout the project.
In a recent community college project, our sponsor reached out to our client to have a tough conversation that they will not make their go live if they continue to delay key decisions. As a result, both sponsors held a weekly 15-minute meeting to address what decisions need to be made and how to help their team overcome the anxiety of making big decisions.
Change Management
A redesign or a comprehensive campaign is about more than a new look or new technologies. All the people, teams, and processes behind the site need to recalibrate and adjust, or sometimes change radically. We, too, are not just about the new look. We understand and incorporate change management into our discovery, reporting, and planning.
For example, one client transitioning from PDFs to a CMS needed support understanding content editing. Though their site wasn’t live, we conducted CMS walk-throughs to build confidence and speed up decision-making.
Another common challenge is using a public website as an intranet. Through audits and our One Source Workshop, we help teams define what content belongs publicly and what doesn’t—establishing governance to prevent internal content from resurfacing on the site.

question: Wireframes/Priority Guides
answer:
The wireframes/priority guide defines content sources (words and pictures), documents the strategy, and weaves intent throughout the design process. The guide sorts your content into a storytelling priority order, without layout specifications. This hierarchy is based on relevance to users, calling out the content most critical to both user satisfaction/needs and organizational goals. This guide shows relationships and priority for navigation and content elements, offering content intent and strategy for each template. We focus on defining prioritization and how your story will be represented.
The format of the content priority guide is not fixed: it can be digital, or it can be physical, on a whiteboard or made with paper and Post-its. Most importantly, a designer’s priority guide is automatically content-first, with a strong focus on providing the best experience for users.
During the content priorities workshops, your strategist will work through the stories your site must tell to achieve your goals. Stories are then mapped to the hierarchy and functionality for an interactive experience. Guides are created for each design template and presented to your team to walk through their features.

question: Client Expectations for Digital/SEM Projects
answer:
Engaging our clients isn’t just about sharing updates—it’s about ensuring your insights, feedback, and priorities shape the campaign’s direction in real time. Here’s how we structure engagement specifically during digital campaign execution:

Campaign Kickoff and Goal Alignment: Before launch, we conduct a collaborative session (virtually or in-person) to define KPIs, identify key messages, align timelines, and assign responsibilities. This sets a clear foundation for performance and accountability.

Content and Creative Previews: We provide clients with previews of digital assets (e.g., ads, landing pages, emails, etc.) for feedback and approval. This ensures the tone, branding, and messaging are on point and reflect your priorities.

Real-Time Dashboard Access: Busy stakeholders want answers, not details. Our analytics include real-time insights via real-time, non-proprietary Looker Studio dashboards and easy-to-share static reports to support ongoing strategy for both paid and organic campaigns.

Monthly Reporting: While we report on KPIs like impressions, clicks, conversions, and engagement by campaign, we plan to spend half of each call discussing the next steps and strategy. During these calls, we want the client to bring insights that help everyone understand the quality of the campaigns’ leads.

Optimization Check-ins: We may schedule quick check-ins (outside regular status calls) during active campaigns when performance shifts significantly. These ad hoc meetings allow us to make timely adjustments with your input, whether reallocating budget, shifting targeting, or refining creative.

Post-Campaign Reviews and Learnings: At the end of each campaign, we conduct a collaborative debrief to evaluate outcomes against original KPIs, assess audience behavior, and identify insights for future campaigns. Continuous improvement is core to our work together.

question: Research Experience to Help Create an Effective Media Strategy
answer:
Research-Driven Media Strategy Expertise

At Stamats, research is the foundation of every effective media strategy we build—particularly in support of enrollment growth for colleges and universities. With decades of experience serving two-year and four-year institutions, including community and technical colleges, we understand the unique demands of higher education marketing and the critical role data plays in driving results.

AI-powered campaign management, including in-auction bidding and machine learning optimization.

Call tracking and automated lead scoring to enhance campaign attribution and refine targeting.

Personalized landing pages and dynamic content aligned to audience interest and intent.

At Stamats, we don’t view media planning in isolation. We consider how every tactic supports the student journey—from early brand awareness through to application and enrollment—and we use research at every stage to ensure we’re reaching the right student, with the right message, at the right time.

Reporting Frequency

Monthly Reports: Stamats will deliver comprehensive campaign performance reports at the end of each month. These reports will include performance summaries, insights, and recommended adjustments. If requested, additional reports can be provided for specific campaigns or milestones, ensuring that [Client Name]remains informed and agile in campaign management.

Milestone Updates: Upon completion of major milestones or campaign phases, Stamats will post summaries detailing work completed, results achieved, and any recommended course corrections.

Key Performance Indicators (KPIs)

Impressions: Total number of times ads are displayed, indicating reach and visibility.

Click-Through Rate (CTR): The ratio of users who click on ads relative to impressions, measuring ad relevance and engagement.

Conversions: Actions defined as goals within the campaigns, such as inquiries, applications, or form submissions, are essential for assessing lead generation and enrollment pipeline development.

Cost Per Lead (CPL): The average cost associated with acquiring a lead, calculated by dividing total spend by the number of leads generated.

Return on Ad Spend (ROAS): Revenue generated from enrolled students attributable to advertising efforts divided by the total ad spend, evaluating financial efficiency.

Enrollment-Related Goals: Tracking the number of campaign-attributed inquiries, applications, and actual enrollments to measure campaign impact on college enrollment objectives directly.

question: Approach to Granular Editing Permissions
answer:
An approach to granular editing permissions—for example, allowing faculty to edit only their own profile pages or submit department news posts.

Stamats offers a tailored approach to user permissions and content governance in WordPress, enabling faculty and staff to manage only the content relevant to their roles, such as editing their profile pages or submitting department-specific news posts.

To support this, we implement role-based access controls within WordPress, using native user roles or custom roles created specifically for Geneseo. These roles can be finely tuned to restrict access at the page, post, or field level, ensuring that users can only view or edit designated content.

For authentication, we support integration with enterprise systems, including Central Authentication System (CAS), Shibboleth, and LDAP, allowing users to log in using their institutional credentials. This not only enhances security but also simplifies user management across departments.

Our web governance counsel includes identity management tools and workflow configuration to align with the university’s editorial processes. Recognizing that Geneseo employs a distributed content authorship model, we will adapt the system to your structure. For example:

Faculty profiles can be locked to individual users or departments, with permissions to update only their own information.

News submissions can be set up through front-end forms or backend access, with editorial workflows for review and publishing.

Finally, the level of permission granularity we recommend will depend on your team’s size, technical capacity, and CMS adoption. We’ll work closely with you to assess these factors and build a sustainable model that ensures flexibility and control over your website content.

question: WP Engine Security Features
answer:
• Daily encrypted backups retained for at least 30 days

• Firewall protection, malware scanning, and intrusion detection

• Password policies aligned with industry standards

WP Engine supports all three requirements, though the specifics may vary slightly by plan level.

WP Engine automatically performs daily backups of your entire WordPress site (files and database), and:

Backups are encrypted both in transit and at rest.

They are retained for 30 days by default.

Users can create manual backups anytime, such as before plugin updates or significant changes.

WP Engine provides a robust security stack that includes:

A web application firewall (WAF) powered by Cloudflare (available on most plans), blocking malicious traffic before it reaches your site.

Automatic malware scanning and removal through its partnership with Sucuri (on select plans).

Intrusion detection and real-time threat monitoring are built into their infrastructure to guard against common WordPress attack vectors like brute force, DDoS, and SQL injection.

While WordPress itself provides basic password handling, WP Engine enhances security by:

Enforcing strong password policies via their platform security settings.

Recommending or requiring two-factor authentication (2FA) for admin access (and offering integrations to enforce it).

Supporting additional password and access controls through plugins or SSO integration (e.g., Azure AD, Okta, or custom SAML providers).

question: Email Campaigns
answer:
Stamats has crafted content for hundreds of enrollment marketing campaigns for institutions of all sizes across the U.S. We understand the nuances of email marketing to prospective students and families. Audience needs are at the core of successful messaging: What do they want and need to know about your institution to make an enrollment decision?

Working closely with your team, we will uncover key information to guide the email creation process, including:

Audience characteristics (e.g., seniors vs. juniors; family decision-influencers, transfers, credential seekers, and more)

The clear, simple message each email should convey

Next steps, such as deadlines, contact information, and what’s next

Design suggestions to properly balance images/video, text, and calls-to-action

Landing pages that continue the conversation and guide users toward matriculation

Headlines and SEO capabilities to improve visibility, performance, and engagement across channels

Right Messaging, Right Time

Working collaboratively with the [Client Name] team, we will match each message’s intent with the intended audience’s unique needs and their specific next step in the enrollment journey. This precision is especially critical when engaging with [Client Name]’s key targets: gig workers, career changers, and nontraditional students—audiences who often face complex barriers to entry and are less likely to follow a traditional path to enrollment.

Our team will create warm, actionable content that captures interest and supports prospective students in taking meaningful steps forward. This includes crafting messages that speak directly to real-life concerns—flexibility, affordability, and support—helping prospects envision how [Client Name] fits into their busy lives.

We can also help [Client Name] address one of its key challenges: converting standing inquiries into enrolled students. Through segmentation, behavior-triggered messaging, and thoughtful calls to action, we guide prospects toward enrollment with fewer conversion gaps and more measurable engagement.

To support this, we will establish a framework that also contributes to tracking conversion points, offering insights into where prospects disengage and how messaging can be refined midstream to improve outcomes. Our approach ensures that the right message lands at the right moment, driving engagement and meaningful conversion.

Finally, we recognize that clear, consistent communication also plays a role in retention. Supportive messaging that sets expectations, builds confidence, and reinforces a sense of belonging contributes to stronger connections, leading to better persistence from semester to semester and year to year.

With this tailored, strategic approach, we will help [Client Name] generate interest and confidently and successfully move students from inquiry to enrollment and beyond.

Example of Email Content Success

After we launched a series of email campaigns with Pima Community College in 2021, we noticed a delightful trend—email recipients clicked the “apply” link in the messages. This is particularly noteworthy because the largest portion of the list was from leads, like a cold call.

Following this data, Stamats created intentional messaging to highlight the apply link. By the end of the recruitment year, we yielded 700+ clicks to apply from emails. By year two, recruitment could claim more than 45% yield of prospects who completed the application from the click-to-apply list, and 37% of those were registered for class.

question: Structure Over Stress: Cascade Makes Content Easy
answer:
Governance, consistency, and multichannel magic

As universities evolve in the digital space, selecting the best CMS is critical to delivering an accessible, efficient, and user-friendly web experience for all key audiences and the teams maintaining and managing the site. While both Cascade and Modern Campus CMS offer robust features tailored for higher education, Cascade provides key advantages in usability, scalability, support, and integration capabilities, making it a more strategic fit for XXX’s long-term website needs.

Benefits of Cascade’s Structured Approach

Content Reuse Across Pages and SitesStructured content can be easily reused across multiple pages, sections, or websites. For example, a faculty bio or tuition rate stored in one location can be displayed on dozens of pages and updated universally with a single edit.

Better Multichannel PublishingWith clearly defined content fields, the same information can be pushed to multiple platforms—your website, mobile apps, digital signage, and more—without reformatting.

Stronger Web Governance and Consistency

Structured fields guide users to enter content correctly and consistently. It eliminates the risk of inconsistent formatting, accidental deletions, or broken layouts that can occur when users rely on WYSIWYG editors.Separation of Content and DesignEditors focus on the content, while designers control how that content is presented. This makes redesigning easier, improves accessibility, and ensures branding consistency without needing content rewrites.

Improved Content Quality and AccessibilityWith defined inputs, content can be more easily validated, checked for accessibility issues, or analyzed for readability and compliance.

Faster Onboarding and Reduced Training Time. With a guided interface and role-based fields, non-technical users can confidently contribute to the website without learning HTML or complex styling.

In Contrast…

Modern Campus CMS’s reliance on a single WYSIWYG editor for much of its content management places a significant burden on content creators to maintain structure and consistency manually. This can lead to formatting issues, governance challenges, and difficulty maintaining content across a distributed team or multisite environment.

question: AI & Future-Proofing
answer:
We build websites with your future in mind. From project kick-off to comprehensive Discovery, we are intentional and thoughtful in our strategy. By coupling your team’s input and aspirations with our proven design system, we break down the site into flexible, scalable assets—modular components that not only keep the experience fresh but also allow your digital team to adapt quickly to new needs and opportunities.

Our goal is to create a platform that is easy to maintain across the entire university ecosystem—not just for the main site, but for individual colleges, schools, departments, centers, and units. As former product owners and webmasters, we understand the importance of sustainability, speed, and usability. We want your team to feel empowered, not overwhelmed.

We also design for what’s next. Your new website will be built with extendibility and innovation to support emerging technologies that elevate user experience and digital strategy.

We will evaluate and utilize new tools and approaches to create compelling digital experiences that invite users to return again and again. We will ensure your site performs reliably, scales with ease, and remains future-ready for new opportunities, challenges, and technologies on the horizon.

question: Interactive Flipbook Component
answer:
As part of the redesign, Stamats will support [Client Name]in selecting an appropriate third-party HTML5-based flipbook tool—an interactive, mobile-friendly solution for presenting digital publications such as viewbooks, magazines, or brochures. We will provide strategic counsel to guide decisions around tool selection and content integration, ensuring the final implementation aligns with the university’s brand, accessibility standards, and user experience goals. This feature will offer an engaging, realistic page-turning experience while seamlessly complementing the overall website design and storytelling approach.

We’ve included a list of some initial options for consideration.

FlippingBook: FlippingBook enables you to convert PDFs into interactive flipbooks. After creating a flipbook, you can easily embed it into your website by copying the provided HTML embed code and pasting it into your site’s HTML. This method is compatible with most content management systems (CMS).

FlipHTML5: FlipHTML5 offers a user-friendly platform to create flipbooks from PDFs. Once your flipbook is ready, you can embed it into your website by copying the generated iframe code and inserting it into your site’s HTML. FlipHTML5 also provides options to customize the embed code for responsiveness and appearance.

Flipsnack: Flipsnack allows you to seamlessly design and embed flipbooks into your website. After publishing your flipbook, you can obtain the embed code and place it into your website’s HTML. Flipsnack also offers customization options for the appearance of embedded flipbooks.

Paperturn: Paperturn lets you easily create flipbooks and embed them into your website. After creating a flipbook, you can copy the provided embed code and paste it into your site’s HTML. Paperturn also offers options to customize the size and appearance of the embedded flipbook.

Instant Flipbook: Instant Flipbook provides a straightforward way to embed flipbooks into your website. After uploading your flipbook to your web hosting, you can use their embed code generator to create a simple iframe code. This code can then be inserted into your website’s HTML to display the flipbook.

question: AI-Powered Chatbot Integration
answer:
While an integrated chatbot can enhance user engagement and streamline access to key information, we recommend partnering with a third-party platform for this functionality rather than building a custom solution within the CMS.

Third-party chatbot providers specialize in delivering AI-powered tools that are purpose-built for higher education. These solutions typically offer:

Advanced conversational AI capable of handling a wide range of student, faculty, and visitor queries

24/7 support and self-service capabilities, reducing the burden on university staff

Seamless integrations with existing systems (e.g., admissions, financial aid, course catalogs, and learning management systems)

Accessibility and compliance features that align with university standards

Data and analytics to track usage, improve response quality, and inform communication strategies

By leveraging a third-party chatbot, [Client Name]can launch a reliable, scalable, and fully supported solution faster and cost-effectively than developing and maintaining a custom in-house tool. This approach also ensures the chatbot remains adaptable as user expectations and technologies evolve.

We’re happy to provide recommendations for vetted vendors and support integration as part of the broader redesign process.

question: Our Migration Approach and Project Plan
answer:
Sitemap Import: Every Page

Creating a migration plan is essential. Without a plan, timelines are at risk. There is also a risk of making inconsistent decisions about migrating different types of content.

Stamats can create a content matrix of every page or work off a file provided. Having a detailed sitemap provides transparency and an easy-to-track progress sheet.

Migration Dashboard

With our Excel file, we will provide a dashboard to easily see a rollup of the project status and assignments. The dashboard can be customized to meet your reporting needs.

If possible, Stamats would like to import the sitemap into WordPress using WP All Import. Sitemap changes can still be adjusted, and our team can link to all pages within the website during migration. It also saves significant setup time and can prefill title and meta-description information. The plug-in can be removed after migration.

Also, if desired, Stamats can install a plug-in to organize WordPress in folders or based on their hierarchy. This provides content administrators with a tree view of their pages.

Migration Planning—It’s More Than Copy and Paste

Our extensive experience with college and university migration projects has prepared us for many different situations. One of the most important is planning for a new design. Commonly, the new site design has specific components for what previously was content. Our detailed mapping document creates rules for migrators to follow. This important work is more than just migration, it is the decision-making that goes into migration, and the skill that Stamats provides. Automated scripts cannot read and detect this level of migration.

Sample Page Set and Decisions

For your project, the two platforms are not the same and neither is the design. Our first sprint is to migrate a sampling of each page type, review them with you, and continue to build on our mapping document. For example, on the current site, the School of Business page is significantly different from the School of Arts and Sciences. If the new design has a Department/School template, decisions will need to be made on how to make both as consistent as possible. Working through a sample of each of these helps the team create a solid plan, reducing reuse, and increasing the quality of the migration.

Once we are aligned, we begin our full-team migration. We manage the work in the detailed content matrix, a living tool used throughout the life cycle of the project.

Migration To the New Design

Following the mapping plan, the Stamats team migrates existing pages to the new layout. Without this level of attention to the migration plan, clients risk:

Missed opportunities to optimize content

Loss of SEO value

Failure to take advantage of your new design features

Working in sprints, the Stamats teams make weekly commitments to a block of migration, holding them accountable and providing confidence that your timeline will be met. No surprises at the end of XXX (MONTH BEFORE CLIENT WANTS TO GO LIVE) that thousands of pages are still outstanding.

Each week, pages will be released for CLIENT NAME to review.

The University of North Carolina Wilmington example below shows an example of an “As Is” Migration, where the content is not simply moved but migrated INTO the components and optimized for the new design. The content is the same, the presentation is significantly improved.

Project Management and Communication

Our plan for Client Name is to scale quickly, ensure efficient communication, and collaborate with your internal teams for mutual success. By distributing workloads among experienced staff members, we efficiently manage complex and expansive projects like this.

Before starting, our teams will review timelines, coordinate resources, plan deliverables, and schedule regular check-ins. Key Stamats personnel will be identified and dedicated to Client Name according to the project schedule. This careful planning ensures a successful partnership, backed by decades of experience—and tens of thousands of pages of migration.

While specific tasks will be assigned at the start of the project, we already anticipate several key points of engagement for Client Name personnel. Your involvement is crucial to the project’s success, and we will rely on continuous feedback and active participation, keeping in mind we will handle the heavy lifting.

As a Value Add, our content migrators understand conversion strategy and page aesthetics. We will maximize the usability and beauty of your pages following your content templates and WordPress’s capabilities.

Quality Review and Reporting

Once migration is complete, we will conduct quality assurance checks that include identifying and resolving issues related to broken links, missing assets, or functionality problems that may arise from the migration process. We recognize this project encompasses a significant number of pages. A robust plan for cleanup and quality assurance ensures a smooth transition to the new WordPress site.

Post Launch Report

Stamats will build a Technical Looker Studio Dashboard that connects to Core Web Vitals. Client Name will own this. The dashboard addresses Core Web Vitals, lists pages by load speed, and tracks hostname and 404s.

question: Website Governance Workshop
answer:
Stamats governance workshop is an interactive session that addresses the three key components of website governance in the context of your specific situation. Together, we review these important three “Ps” to set the foundation for ongoing, stable governance that will best serve the institution for keeping the site up to date and achieving your goals.

Our focus is the future state and the change management needed to get there. We start by considering the current state—mostly to measure the work needed to get to the desired future state. We look at:

Decision-making authority

Governance maturity

Workflow decisions

Policies

We address key topics such as defining the difference between web governance and content governance, understanding how governance currently works within your organizational culture, and who controls content.

Importantly, we work with the team to answer these key questions regarding governance decisions around:

Who: People, roles, and offices with authority

How: Policies, standards, and guides (as well as the tools for workflows, QA checks, etc.)

When: Calendars, deadlines, and cycles

Why: Shared goals

question: Expectations/contribution's of employees for the website redesign project.
answer:
In general, our expectations of a client during a website redesign can be summarized as follows:

Communicate continually. It’s the best way to ensure we are all in sync and moving toward the same goals--big and small--throughout the project. Our project managers will work hard to maintain continual communication with you, but we welcome any and all phone calls, Zoom/Microsoft Teams meetings, emails, or any other form of communication that helps us all stay connected and on target.

Ask questions. Wonder how we will analyze your existing data? Or why a schedule doesn’t have a task listed? Or maybe why we didn’t recommend an events calendar plug-in? Just ask. We’ll be happy to answer. And if we don’t know, we’ll find out.

Be candid. If you have a concern at any point along the way, just let us know. If you need further explanation on what we are doing, if you don’t feel you can meet a deadline, even if you just aren’t completely comfortable with an idea, bring it up. We appreciate openness.

Give us access to the people and the platforms that power the website. We will need to look under the hood of your analytics. We’ll need to talk to the people who know how your CRM or SIS interact with the site. We’ll need conversations with your content editors. We may need to hear from students or faculty. Brand assets. Photo libraries. Style manuals.

Be clear about decisions. Many decisions go into a new site. Let us know who has final say—or, let us know you need our help to figure that out. We do that, too, through our governance consultation.

question: Describe how site cookie notifications are handled.
answer:
We design cookie notices to conform to UX conventions and your custom UI. By UX conventions, we mean they usually appear near the lower edge of the viewport to distinguish from alerts, and, if chat is deployed, then cookie notices shift to the lower left.

With regard to cookie policies, Stamats does not provide legal advice on privacy policies or practices. We do consult on certain aspects of information privacy, such as data protection, handling sensitive data, and common practices that we believe meet regulatory requirements. We recommend you engage appropriate legal counsel to review all privacy policies and practices.

question: Content Approval Workflow Integrated Across Different CMS Systems
answer:
Stamats was a pioneer in developing workflows before plug-ins were available with custom coding of owner, date, and revision cycle. While we still have that code, new plug-ins are available that allow workflow control down to a specific element on the page. Stamats will work with you to evaluate the plug-in options and configure the workflow process. Workflow can include multi-level approvals/rejections, email notifications, and page scheduling. Administrators can enable automated and preconfigured, customized, or on-the-fly workflow. Each user has their own personal workflow tracker and mailbox to quickly view and access checked-out pages and monitor projects within each workflow.

An administrator can set up a workflow using different users as approvers. The approver will be sent any notifications for pages in the workflow. Notified users typically have higher privileges in the workflow process such as editing, reviewing, publishing, and approving files. There can be multiple approvers that a page must iterate through, which provides a hierarchy of approval.

question: Testing; Including Technical Functionality and Content Validation
answer:
In preparation for testing, Stamats will migrate 20 sample pages (under the migration plan). This provides a huge value add because not only is it migrating 20 pages, but it is also setting up the core library structure for the components that go on the page. If you have a program filter, we will need a minimum of five written program pages provided to test the program filter.

Extensive browser and responsive testing would include:

Test all templates and the program filter functionality

Browser testing in Firefox, Edge, Chrome, Safari, iPhone, and Android for the latest version or last two versions

Testing on various sizes/devices; up to three desktop/tablet sizes

Enter and document all bugs into a bug tracking system

Manage bugs and corrections

Retesting bug fixes while confirming the changes pass accessibility

Our testers have extensive WordPress experience and have tested dozens and dozens of sites. They are also trained in accessibility and web best practices to provide a holistic review of the site.

All testing is documented with screenshots, videos, or tasks taken.

question: Intuitive User Experience
answer:
We know that the overall journey is a critical component of the user experience. Mapping the overall flow of the user experience can expose trouble spots or opportunities where the user may not have a clear path. Stamats assesses user paths and conducts mystery shopping to document overall journey experiences. The assessment will include three user journey evaluations to identify experience and conversion barriers and to optimize touchpoint and engagement opportunities.

We typically evaluate journey experience for three key components:

Consideration: The task or question that brings them to the site, as well as the decisions(s) they make to continue to the second phase

Clarification: The further questions they ask while on the site, as well as the decisions(s) they make to continue to the second phase

Conversion: The phase where this journey ends and the next begins—for students, inquiry, application, or registration

question: Describe your system or process to ensure fast page load speeds.
answer:
Stamats follows an agile process which includes testing throughout the stages of the redesign and then on the final hosting server for speed performance and site experience.

One of the keys to site speed is image optimization. Stamats can provide counsel and services to optimize images for download as well as video compression.

When developing our HTML and code, we use minified files, only necessary and optimized third-party plugins, caching strategies, and lightweight parent themes when applicable. We also make sure we code for mobile first to optimize content.

At launch, Stamats provides a free post- go-live analysis report that includes speed performance among other categories.

question: Current Website Challenges & Opportunities
answer:
THESE ARE TYPICAL ISSUES--1) Missing the selling opportunity; 2) Too many PDFs; 3) Updating/accessible content (and governance).

From an outside view, the site is missing the sales opportunity of your great programs and educational experience. The home page dedicates a large portion of the page to news and events as well as available actions but lacks persuasive messaging as to why to choose [Client Name]. If a prospective student is evaluating whether you are a good fit, the site doesn’t support their evaluation process.

The program pages are missing the differentiators of your programs. They cover the facts of the programs, and like the home page, they lack the why. If a prospective student asked you about your pilot program, it is unlikely that you would read the content on the website. Instead, you would most likely describe the excellent program and what makes it special at [Client Name]. The website is often the first point of evaluation and therefore must be a key part of your marketing plan.

The site has over 1,000 PDFs which is not an ideal way to present content. PDFs often do not pass accessibility, putting you at risk. While your RFP did not address PDFs, this will be a critical part of your content and accessibility strategy.

With over 25 content contributors, we can assume there is an ongoing challenge of keeping the team updated in content and accessibility requirements. Our reusable asset strategy and content templates are designed specifically to help colleges with dozens of users provide a consistent experience. This inconsistency can impact your site performance. The current site has over 1,000 images that are considered significantly large causing a slow site performance. One of those images— https://sunysccc.edu/images/news/FeedAlbany7.png—is almost an MG when it should be less than 100 KB.

question: Information Architecture and Content Strategy: Taxonomy (Optional)
answer:
Based on your information architecture, wireframes, and page layout structure, taxonomy can, and should, play an important part of the content strategy. Taxonomy provides the foundation for dynamic pages that automatically display current, relevant content. In our strategy, we’ll identify types of content that should be shared and connected (e.g., news stories, event details, faculty bios, related programs, and testimonials) across your site in multiple areas. We’ll also define tags (academic programs, audience types, courses of interest, etc.) that help deliver your content to the right pages and the right people.

Taxonomy enables you to do the following—all of which we find are goals among our college and university clients:

Dynamically bring content together for the visitor experience instead of keeping it siloed

Centralize types of content for governance (e.g., news in the news section) dynamically rather than manually adding content to individual pages

Reduce the risk of outdated and duplicated content

We do this by creating content types and taxonomy that connect related content. A taxonomy strategy consists of a centralized classification of content that all types use. For example, academic programs would be mapped to news categories, events categories, testimonials, faculty bios, and delivery options.

Done right, taxonomy lets you automatically populate a course page with student outcomes or an office page with current contacts. It keeps pages sitewide fresh and accurate.

Another dynamic element is cloning, which allows content to live within multiple sections and appear within the appropriate context. You might have a directions page that must be referenced in each course. Instead of jumping users out to a location section and changing the navigation, the content can live within that section. This can have SEO implications if not properly designed and built. Stamats understands these complexities and how to handle content as part of your content strategy.

Taxonomy will inform design and development of modular content elements that make up your new site’s visual hierarchy.

question: Governance Audit, Plan, and Implementation (Optional)
answer:
Governance Audit, Plan, and Implementation

The best time to implement a governance plan is during a website redesign. An ungoverned site can cost you time and money, erode your brand, and negatively impact user experience. A Stamats governance plan provides a structure for how content is created, by whom, and how it is distributed, and protects the integrity of your site and the work that has been done.

A governance plan:

Defines which sites are part of the plan (e.g., microsites, third-party sites, etc.)

Crafts roles and responsibilities for web content maintenance

Assigns refresh cycles and owners

Creates policies to maintain content integrity

Provides guidelines for all types of content

Assigns content and assets to a role, a process, and a schedule

Creates a monitoring program for system-wide governance (e.g., broken links, accessibility compliance, image format, etc.) and individual assignments for content review

Establishes reporting expectations

Communicates changes

Our governance workshops are led by strategists with on-campus experience at complex institutions who understand the importance of buy-in and matching governance to institutional culture. We focus our plans on creating a sustainable solution that the institution believes in and supports.
